vocabulary.definition: n. 集合, 聚集；[法] 集会, 人一, 红衣主教会议
vocabulary.context: A murmur of awe and praise rises from the congregation when the blast finally ends.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当爆炸最终结束时，会众中响起了敬畏和赞美的低语

vocabulary.definition: adj. 欢喜的, 高兴的, 令人高兴的
vocabulary.context: Rosh Hashanah is a joyful holiday, but it is also a serious one.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 犹太新年是一个欢乐的节日，但也是一个严肃的节日

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使能够；[计] 允许, 使能, 打开
vocabulary.context: Next, we recite the Shehecheyanu (sheh-heh-hee-YAH-noo), a blessing thanking God for enabling us to celebrate this special occasion.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 接下来，我们背诵 Shehecheyanu (sheh-heh-hee-YAH-noo)，这是感谢上帝使我们能够庆祝这个特殊时刻的祝福

vocabulary.definition: adv. 激动地, 刺激地, 令人兴奋地
vocabulary.context: Perhaps most stirringly, the Black opera soprano Marian Anderson sang "He's Got the Whole World in His Hands." Twenty-three years earlier, Anderson had been barred from appearing at Constitution Hall in Washington, D.C., because she was Black.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 也许最激动人心的是黑人歌剧女高音玛丽安·安德森演唱的"他手中掌握着整个世界"。二十三年前，安德森被禁止出现在华盛顿特区的宪法大厅，因为她是黑人

vocabulary.definition: n. 女高音, 最高音部, 女高音歌手；adj. 女高音的
vocabulary.context: Perhaps most stirringly, the Black opera soprano Marian Anderson sang "He's Got the Whole World in His Hands." Twenty-three years earlier, Anderson had been barred from appearing at Constitution Hall in Washington, D.C., because she was Black.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 也许最激动人心的是黑人歌剧女高音玛丽安·安德森演唱的"他手中掌握着整个世界"。二十三年前，安德森被禁止出现在华盛顿特区的宪法大厅，因为她是黑人

vocabulary.definition: n. 四重奏；[计] 四位字节
vocabulary.context: As people gathered on the Mall, Black folk artist Odetta sang, "I'm On My Way to Freedom Land." The white folk trio Peter, Paul, and Mary sang Bob Dylan's "Blowin' in the Wind." The Albany Freedom Singers, a Black quartet, performed "We Shall Not Be Moved." Baez and Dylan sang "Keep Your Eyes on the Prize."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当人们聚集在购物中心时，黑人民间艺术家奥黛塔唱道："我正在通往自由之地的路上。"白人民谣三人组彼得、保罗和玛丽演唱了鲍勃·迪伦的《在风中飘》。黑人四重奏奥尔巴尼自由歌手演唱了《我们不会被感动》。贝兹和迪伦演唱了"保持关注奖品"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 高压的, 高气压的, 强行的；v.t. 施以高压, 强迫
vocabulary.context: Singing inspired activists to stand up to the verbal and physical assaults, to the police dogs, and to the high-pressure fire hoses aimed at them.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 歌声激励活动人士勇敢地面对言语和身体攻击、警犬和针对他们的高压消防水带

vocabulary.definition: n. 圣歌, 赞美诗
vocabulary.context: Two gospel songs - "I'll Overcome Someday" and "If My Jesus Wills" - provided the basis for this unofficial civil rights anthem.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 两首福音歌曲--《有一天我会克服》和《如果我的耶稣愿意》--为这首非官方的民权赞歌提供了基础

vocabulary.definition: adj. 圣经的
vocabulary.context: "Joshua Fit the Battle of Jericho" is a traditional African American spiritual that tells the biblical story of a man who brought down the walled city of Jericho despite overwhelming odds.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 《约书亚适合杰里科之战》是一部传统的非裔美国人灵歌，讲述了一个男人不顾一切困难，攻陷了城墙围城杰里科的圣经故事

vocabulary.definition: adj. 用言辞的, 言语的, 口头的, 逐字的, 动词的；[医] 言语的, 口述的
vocabulary.context: Singing inspired activists to stand up to the verbal and physical assaults, to the police dogs, and to the high-pressure fire hoses aimed at them.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 歌声激励活动人士勇敢地面对言语和身体攻击、警犬和针对他们的高压消防水带

vocabulary.definition: n. 福音, 信仰, 真理
vocabulary.context: Two gospel songs - "I'll Overcome Someday" and "If My Jesus Wills" - provided the basis for this unofficial civil rights anthem.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 两首福音歌曲--《有一天我会克服》和《如果我的耶稣愿意》--为这首非官方的民权赞歌提供了基础

vocabulary.definition: n. 反对, 敌对, 相反, 在野党；[医] 对生, 对向, 反抗, 反对症
vocabulary.context: Its text reaffirmed the movement's goals: to stand united against all obstacles to freedom and equality, to face opposition with courage and strength, and to live in peace.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其文本重申了该运动的目标：团结起来反对自由和平等的一切障碍，以勇气和力量面对反对，并和平生活

vocabulary.definition: n. 柑橘属果树
vocabulary.context: Then, beekeepers go south to pollinate citrus trees in Florida.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然后，养蜂人南下佛罗里达州为柑橘树授粉

vocabulary.definition: n. 授粉；[医] 授粉[作用], 传粉[作用]
vocabulary.context: Beekeepers transport their colonies up the east coast to work on apple, peach, and blueberries pollination in the summer.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 养蜂人将蜂群运送到东海岸，在夏季为苹果、桃子和蓝莓授粉

vocabulary.definition: n. 鳄梨, 鳄梨树
vocabulary.context: They help produce foods like apples, watermelon, peppers, broccoli, peaches, berries, avocados, and even almonds.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它们有助于生产苹果、西瓜、辣椒、西兰花、桃子、浆果、鳄梨，甚至杏仁等食物

vocabulary.definition: adv. 无意中, 非存心地, 不知不觉地
vocabulary.context: By buzzing between plants, bees unknowingly move pollen from one plant to another.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 通过在植物之间嗡嗡作响，蜜蜂不知不觉地将花粉从一株植物转移到另一株植物

vocabulary.definition: adj. 麻烦的, 恼人的, 棘手的, 讨厌的
vocabulary.context: Bees may seem pesky - buzzing around sugary snacks, hiding in grasses, and defending with stings that inject itchy venom.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 蜜蜂可能看起来很讨厌--在含糖零食周围嗡嗡作响，躲在草丛中，并用注入发痒毒液的蜇来进行防御

vocabulary.definition: n. 花椰菜
vocabulary.context: They help produce foods like apples, watermelon, peppers, broccoli, peaches, berries, avocados, and even almonds.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它们有助于生产苹果、西瓜、辣椒、西兰花、桃子、浆果、鳄梨，甚至杏仁等食物

vocabulary.definition: n. 剂量, 服用量；v.t. 给药, (用药)医治；v.i. 服药
vocabulary.context: Scientists are figuring out how to replace harmful pesticides with more bee-friendly chemicals and how to spray pesticides in smaller doses, so that pesticides do not kill bees or the food they eat.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 科学家们正在研究如何用对蜜蜂更友好的化学物质取代有害的农药，以及如何以较小的剂量喷洒农药，这样农药就不会杀死蜜蜂或它们吃的食物

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 培养, 耕作
vocabulary.context: Additionally, beekeepers and scientists encourage people to cultivate habitats that are healthy for bees.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 此外，养蜂人和科学家鼓励人们培育对蜜蜂健康的栖息地

vocabulary.definition: n. 生产品, 物产, 后代；v.t. 产生, 生产, 提出, 出示；v.i. 生产, 制造
vocabulary.context: They help produce foods like apples, watermelon, peppers, broccoli, peaches, berries, avocados, and even almonds.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它们有助于生产苹果、西瓜、辣椒、西兰花、桃子、浆果、鳄梨，甚至杏仁等食物

vocabulary.definition: n. 牙痛，牙疼( toothache的名词复数 )
vocabulary.context: For thousands of years, traditional Chinese medicine has depended on tiger parts from across Asia to treat illnesses: people believed noses treated epilepsy, tiger bones healed ulcers, tiger tails cured skin diseases, and tiger whiskers soothed toothaches.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 几千年来，传统中医一直依靠亚洲各地的虎部位来治疗疾病：人们相信鼻子可以治疗癫痫，虎骨可以治疗溃疡，虎尾可以治疗皮肤病，虎须可以缓解牙痛

vocabulary.definition: n. 动物群；[医] 动物区系
vocabulary.context: As poaching of Asia's tigers got out of control, CITES (Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora) banned the trade of tiger parts in 1987.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 随着亚洲虎偷猎失控，CITES（濒危野生动植物种国际贸易公约）于1987年禁止老虎器官贸易

vocabulary.definition: n. 溃疡, 腐烂物；[医] 溃疡
vocabulary.context: For thousands of years, traditional Chinese medicine has depended on tiger parts from across Asia to treat illnesses: people believed noses treated epilepsy, tiger bones healed ulcers, tiger tails cured skin diseases, and tiger whiskers soothed toothaches.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 几千年来，传统中医一直依靠亚洲各地的虎部位来治疗疾病：人们相信鼻子可以治疗癫痫，虎骨可以治疗溃疡，虎尾可以治疗皮肤病，虎须可以缓解牙痛

vocabulary.definition: n. 鬃毛
vocabulary.context: Shaggy manes and thick coats of fur keep out the chill.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 蓬松的鬃毛和厚厚的毛皮可以御寒

vocabulary.definition: n. 植物群；[医] 植物区系, 植物丛, 植物群, 菌丛
vocabulary.context: As poaching of Asia's tigers got out of control, CITES (Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora) banned the trade of tiger parts in 1987.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 随着亚洲虎偷猎失控，CITES（濒危野生动植物种国际贸易公约）于1987年禁止老虎器官贸易

vocabulary.definition: n. 炫耀, 飘扬；v. 炫耀, 飘扬
vocabulary.context: Think of displaying a skinned tiger like flaunting a fancy sports car.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 想象一下展示一只被剥皮的老虎就像炫耀一辆豪华跑车一样

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不可抵抗的, 不能压制的；[经] 不可抗力
vocabulary.context: Although snuggling a tiger cub sounds irresistible, tourists' money fuels tigers' captivity.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管依偎着一只小虎听起来令人难以抗拒，但游客的金钱却助长了老虎的圈养

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有野心的, 抱负不凡的, 雄心勃勃的
vocabulary.context: Although countries did not reach this ambitious goal, the future looks hopeful for tigers.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管各国没有实现这一雄心勃勃的目标，但老虎的未来充满希望

vocabulary.definition: n. 傻瓜, 笨蛋
vocabulary.context: Loggerhead turtles eat plastic bags thinking they are jellyfish.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 红海龟以为自己是水母而吃塑料袋

vocabulary.definition: n. 信天翁, 沉重负担
vocabulary.context: Plastics also kill up to a million seabirds each year, including the Laysan Albatross.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 塑料每年还会杀死多达一百万只海鸟，其中包括莱桑信天翁

vocabulary.definition: adj. 能进行生物降解的
vocabulary.context: Plastics are not biodegradable, meaning they do not decompose.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 塑料不可生物降解，这意味着它们不会分解

vocabulary.definition: n. 环保人士
vocabulary.context: Although there is not a simple answer, scientists and environmentalists are developing new ideas to address the plastic problem.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然没有一个简单的答案，但科学家和环保主义者正在开发新的想法来解决塑料问题

vocabulary.definition: n. 拾起, 学会, 偶然结识, 好转, 刺激品, 拾波, 电视摄像, 拾音器, 唱头, 拾音
vocabulary.context: By incentivizing plastic pick-ups, The Plastic Bank prevents these materials from ending up in the ocean and recycles the collected plastics into new products.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 通过激励塑料回收，塑料银行防止这些材料最终流入海洋，并将收集到的塑料回收成新产品

vocabulary.definition: n. 翼展
vocabulary.context: With a wingspan of over six feet, the large seabird flies over oceans and skims its beak across the top of the water to catch fish.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这种大型海鸟的翼展超过六英尺，飞越海洋，用喙掠过水面捕鱼

vocabulary.definition: adj. 可比较的, 比得上的
vocabulary.context: This is comparable to a garbage truck dumping a load full of debris into the sea every minute.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这相当于一辆垃圾车每分钟将满载碎片倾倒到海里

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 推荐, 介绍, 劝告, 使受欢迎, 托付；[经] 建议, 推荐
vocabulary.context: Environmentalists also recommend that citizens can take action to decrease their consumption of ever-convenient plastics.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 环保主义者还建议公民采取行动减少对便利塑料的消耗

vocabulary.definition: adj. 没有被污染的, 未沾染的
vocabulary.context: Milkers follow strict procedures when extracting venom so that the poison stays uncontaminated and so that the milker stays safe.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 挤奶工在提取毒液时遵循严格的程序，以确保毒物不被污染，从而确保挤奶工的安全

vocabulary.definition: n. 爬行动物学；[医] 爬虫学
vocabulary.context: To become a professional snake milker, students need to attend college and study biology, chemistry, or herpetology, the study of reptiles.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 要成为一名专业的蛇挤奶工，学生需要上大学并学习生物学、化学或爬行动物学（爬行动物的研究）

vocabulary.definition: n. 蛇类展出馆
vocabulary.context: Between extractions, milkers maintain the lab equipment used during milking and care for the snakes living in the serpentarium.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在提取之间，挤奶工维护挤奶期间使用的实验室设备，并照顾生活在蛇馆中的蛇

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 冻干
vocabulary.context: After extracting the potent venom, milkers freeze-dry the substance to store and transport the venom to researchers, labs, and hospitals.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 提取强效毒液后，挤奶工将物质冷冻干燥以储存毒液并将其运输到研究人员、实验室和医院

vocabulary.definition: n. 树眼镜蛇；[医] 窄头眼镜蛇(南非产)
vocabulary.context: You'd have to be adventurous and love snakes to put your fingers inches from the fangs of vipers, cobras, mambas, and rattlesnakes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 你必须具有冒险精神并且热爱蛇，才能将手指放在距毒蛇、眼镜蛇、曼巴蛇和响尾蛇的毒牙几英寸的地方

vocabulary.definition: n. 挤奶人, 挤奶器, 乳牛
vocabulary.context: Milkers follow strict procedures when extracting venom so that the poison stays uncontaminated and so that the milker stays safe.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 挤奶工在提取毒液时遵循严格的程序，以确保毒物不被污染，从而确保挤奶工的安全

vocabulary.definition: n. 蛇咬伤
vocabulary.context: This country has the world's highest snakebite death rate, with many victims being children.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这个国家是世界上蛇咬伤死亡率最高的国家，许多受害者是儿童

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有方法的, 有系统的
vocabulary.context: Venom milkers tend to be curious, methodical, and adventurous.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 毒液挤奶工往往充满好奇心、有条理且富有冒险精神

vocabulary.definition: adj. 治病的, 有疗效的；n. 药物, 治疗法
vocabulary.context: Harrison's milking is motivated by his belief in the curative power of snake venom and a deep desire to help others.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 哈里森挤奶的动机是他相信蛇毒的疗效以及帮助他人的强烈愿望

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使安乐死,对…施无痛致死术
vocabulary.context: Every year, wildlife managers euthanize dozens of bears that disturb property in search of food, giving meaning to the saying "a fed bear is a dead bear." Montana Public Radio reported that 46 grizzly bears were killed and five bears relocated due to human-bear conflict in 2018 alone.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 每年，野生动物管理者都会对数十只扰乱财产寻找食物的熊实施安乐死，这句话印证了"吃饱的熊就是死熊"这句话。蒙大拿州公共广播电台报道称，仅 2018 年就有 46 只灰熊被杀死，5 只灰熊因人熊冲突而被转移

vocabulary.definition: adj. 五重的, 五倍的；adv. 五重地, 五倍地
vocabulary.context: Grizzly bears shine as a conservation success story with their numbers increasing fivefold since 1975.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 灰熊的数量自 1975 年以来增加了五倍，成为保护成功的典范

vocabulary.definition: adj. <美>（外援）非武器性的
vocabulary.context: The Montana Bear Education Working Group hands out bear spray, a non-lethal spray that sends bears running, at community events.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 蒙大拿州熊教育工作组在社区活动中分发防熊喷雾剂，这是一种让熊奔跑的非致命喷雾剂

vocabulary.definition: adj. 讨厌的
vocabulary.context: Many westerners that live in grizzly territory consider the bears aggressive and bothersome.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 许多生活在灰熊领地的西方人认为熊具有攻击性且令人烦恼

vocabulary.definition: n. 共存, 和平共处, 并立
vocabulary.context: Striving for coexistence means first changing human thoughts and feelings about bears.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 争取共存意味着首先要改变人类对熊的想法和感受

vocabulary.definition: adj. 邻近的, 接近的；[医] 接触的
vocabulary.context: According to National Geographic , between 1,400 and 1,700 grizzly bears now inhabit the contiguous United States.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 据《国家地理》报道，目前美国本土栖息着 1,400 至 1,700 只灰熊

vocabulary.definition: n. 狭小空间, 鸡笼, 捕鱼篓；v.t. 关进鸡舍, 监禁
vocabulary.context: But their noses and appetites get them into trouble when they raid campsites, livestock pastures, chicken coops, and backyard trash cans.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但是，当它们袭击露营地、牲畜牧场、鸡舍和后院垃圾桶时，它们的鼻子和胃口会给它们带来麻烦

vocabulary.definition: n. 朝西的方向；adj. 向西的；adv. 向西的
vocabulary.context: But as Westward Expansion brought more people west during the 1800s and 1900s, people transformed bear country.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但在 1800 年代和 1900 年代，随着西进运动将更多的人带到了西部，人们改变了熊的国家

vocabulary.definition: adv. 逐渐地
vocabulary.context: Obedience trainers have a deep understanding of dog behavior and use rewards and practice to train dogs incrementally or break bad habits.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 服从训练员对狗的行为有深入的了解，并利用奖励和实践来逐步训练狗或改掉坏习惯

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 服水土；适应新环境；v.t. 使适应；使服水土
vocabulary.context: Trainers prepare animals for all kinds of special jobs, from working with the disabled, performing for audiences, acclimating wild animals to zoo life, and teaching pets obedience.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 训练员帮助动物做好各种特殊工作的准备，从与残疾人一起工作、为观众表演、让野生动物适应动物园生活以及教导宠物服从

vocabulary.definition: adj. 在职的
vocabulary.context: But she admits most of her learning came through on-the-job experience.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但她承认她的大部分学习都是通过工作经验获得的

vocabulary.definition: v. （防倒转的）棘齿( ratchet的过去式和过去分词 )
vocabulary.context: As Halo's searching skills grew, Labrada ratcheted up the difficulty.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 随着 Halo 搜索技能的提高，Labrada 也提高了难度

vocabulary.definition: n. 联合主演；v.t. （指电影等）由（某明星）与其他明星联合主演；v.i. （与某人）联合主演, 合演
vocabulary.context: And the trainers had to help them build a fast friendship with co-star Abigail Breslin, who played Nim.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 培训师必须帮助他们与饰演尼姆的联合主演阿比盖尔·布雷斯林建立快速的友谊

vocabulary.definition: adj. 爱调戏的, 轻浮的
vocabulary.context: The two sea lions, who together played the role of Selkie, arrived trained and ready to woo audiences with flipper waves, flirtatious eyes, and blown kisses.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 两只海狮一起扮演塞尔基的角色，它们经过训练抵达现场，准备用鳍状肢挥动、调情的眼神和飞吻来吸引观众

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 精选, 用手采摘
vocabulary.context: So when Halo was just a few months old, Fire Captain Cat Labrada handpicked Halo to train as a Florida Task Force 1 search and rescue dog.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因此，当 Halo 才几个月大时，消防队长 Cat Labrada 亲自挑选 Halo 作为佛罗里达第一特遣队搜救犬进行训练

vocabulary.definition: n. 示范, 实证；[医] 示教, 实物教授
vocabulary.context: In addition, zoo trainers feed and exercise animals, clean their habitats, keep records of their behavior, and lead animal demonstrations to educate visitors.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 此外，动物园训练员喂养和锻炼动物，清洁它们的栖息地，记录它们的行为，并进行动物演示以教育游客

vocabulary.definition: adj. 鲸脂的, 多鲸脂的
vocabulary.context: Less cod and halibut, fewer blubbery pounds for the narwhal.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 鳕鱼和大比目鱼减少，独角鲸的脂肪磅数减少

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使发声, 使发成元音；v.i. 发声, 练声, 元音化
vocabulary.context: The researchers observed that when noisy ships passed through the Arctic, Nemo fell silent-no vocalizing to communicate or hunt-for 24 hours.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 研究人员观察到，当嘈杂的船只经过北极时，尼莫会陷入沉默--24小时内不再发声进行交流或捕猎

vocabulary.definition: adj. 冰的, 冰川的, 冰川时代的, 冰冷的；[医] 冰的, 冰样的
vocabulary.context: Measuring water temperatures and glacial melting costs scientists lots of time and money.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 测量水温和冰川融化花费了科学家大量的时间和金钱

vocabulary.definition: adv. 引人发笑地；滑稽地；喜不自禁地
vocabulary.context: Later in 2018, she partnered with NASA's Oceans Melting (hilariously nicknamed Project OMG) to track the impacts of global warming.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 2018 年晚些时候，她与 NASA 的 Oceans Melting（绰号为"OMG 计划"）合作追踪全球变暖的影响

vocabulary.definition: adj. 饮食的；n. 规定食物
vocabulary.context: Scientists hypothesize that this dietary shift is a result of climate change.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 科学家推测这种饮食变化是气候变化的结果

vocabulary.definition: adj. 肥胖的, 脂肪的, 含脂肪的；[医] 脂肪的
vocabulary.context: They dive and hunt fatty fish that live in the ice-covered waters and shield themselves from predators in the Bay that is 95% covered in ice.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它们潜水并捕猎生活在冰雪覆盖的水域中的多脂鱼类，并在 95% 被冰雪覆盖的海湾中保护自己免受掠食者的侵害

vocabulary.definition: adj. 常年的, 长期的, 反复的；[医] 多年生的
vocabulary.context: Over the last 25 years, Baffin Bay's perennial ice (ice that remains all year and never melts) shrunk by 9% each decade, according to Science Buzz .
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 据《Science Buzz》报道，在过去 25 年里，巴芬湾的常年冰（常年存在且永不融化的冰）每十年缩小 9%

vocabulary.definition: n. 温度计, 体温计；[化] 温度计
vocabulary.context: Laidre and her team attached tags with thermometers to fourteen narwhals.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 莱德尔和她的团队给十四头独角鲸贴上了带有温度计的标签

vocabulary.definition: n. 生物圈
vocabulary.context: In opposition to the paper released by scientists proposing how to terraform Mars, another scientist wrote a paper detailing a different option to live in low-orbiting man-made biospheres with artificial gravity, as a small step towards living in space.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 与科学家发表的提出如何改造火星的论文相反，另一位科学家写了一篇论文，详细介绍了在具有人工重力的低轨道人造生物圈中生活的不同选择，作为迈向太空生活的一小步

vocabulary.definition: adj. 生活过得有价值的, (生活)过得去的, 值得过的, 适于居住的
vocabulary.context: To terraform Mars into a liveable place for humans, we would heat the environment by pumping out lots of carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases, similar to how we are unintentionally warming Earth currently.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 为了将火星改造为适合人类居住的地方，我们将通过排放大量二氧化碳和其他温室气体来加热环境，类似于我们目前无意中使地球变暖的方式

vocabulary.definition: adj. 生活过得有价值的, (生活)过得去的, 值得过的, 适于居住的
vocabulary.context: To terraform means to transform the planet into something that is livable and replicates the conditions of Earth.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 地球化意味着将地球改造为适合居住并复制地球条件的星球

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未勘查过的
vocabulary.context: For others, the desire propelling humans into space is simply one to push our limits and explore the unexplored of the cosmos.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对于其他人来说，推动人类进入太空的愿望只是突破我们的极限并探索宇宙的未知领域

vocabulary.definition: adv. 激烈地, 彻底地
vocabulary.context: To make Mars livable for humans, we would have to drastically alter the environment, according to PBS.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 据美国公共广播公司报道，为了让火星适合人类居住，我们必须彻底改变环境

vocabulary.definition: n. 宇宙, 秩序, 和谐, 大波斯菊；[医] 大波斯菊
vocabulary.context: For others, the desire propelling humans into space is simply one to push our limits and explore the unexplored of the cosmos.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对于其他人来说，推动人类进入太空的愿望只是突破我们的极限并探索宇宙的未知领域

vocabulary.definition: n. 密度；[化] 密度
vocabulary.context: Our muscles would deteriorate and our bone density would decrease from this lighter gravity.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 由于重力较轻，我们的肌肉会退化，骨密度也会降低

vocabulary.definition: adj. 合乎逻辑的, 合理的；[法] 逻辑的, 符合逻辑的
vocabulary.context: The most logical choice of planet to live on would be Mars, the closest planet to Earth and one we are in the process of exploring.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 最合乎逻辑的居住行星选择是火星，它是距离地球最近的行星，也是我们正在探索的行星

vocabulary.definition: n. 根除者, 扑灭者, 害虫驱除剂
vocabulary.context: Although cane toads were poor beetle exterminators, they were excellent at multiplying.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管甘蔗蟾蜍的甲虫消灭能力较差，但它们的繁殖能力却非常出色

vocabulary.definition: n. 澳洲的一种野狗( dingo的复数形式 )
vocabulary.context: But larger predators in Australia - lizards, crocodiles, turtles, snakes, and dingoes - have no resistance to the poison and fall victim to its heart-seizing effects.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但澳大利亚的大型食肉动物--蜥蜴、鳄鱼、海龟、蛇和野狗--对这种毒药没有抵抗力，并成为其令人心惊胆战的受害者

vocabulary.definition: n. 放毒人, 毒死他人者；[法] 投毒者, 毒害者, 毒物
vocabulary.context: The predators got momentarily sick - kind of like food poisoning - but built up immunity to the toad's toxin and learned to avoid eating adult toads which are the most powerful poisoners.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 捕食者暂时生病了--有点像食物中毒--但增强了对蟾蜍毒素的免疫力，并学会了避免吃成年蟾蜍，这是最强大的毒物

vocabulary.definition: n. 自助餐, 小卖部, 碗橱, 殴打, 冲击；v.t. 连续打, 冲击, 搏斗；v.i. 奋斗
vocabulary.context: Their Australian home is like an all-you-can-eat buffet.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们的澳大利亚之家就像是自助餐

vocabulary.definition: adv. 无法控制地
vocabulary.context: Brought to Australia to kill agricultural pests, cane toads spread uncontrollably and quickly became the pest.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 甘蔗蟾蜍被带到澳大利亚杀死农业害虫，但其蔓延不受控制，很快成为害虫

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 发泄(怒火), 报仇；[法] 报仇, 报复, 施
vocabulary.context: But the cane toad's superpower - or defense mechanism in biological terms - has wreaked havoc in northern Australia.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但甘蔗蟾蜍的超能力--或者生物学意义上的防御机制--已经在澳大利亚北部造成了严重破坏

vocabulary.definition: n. 荒野, 荒地；adj. 野性的, 野蛮的, 野生的, 失控的, 任性的, 杂乱的, 轻率的, 狂热的, 疯狂的；adv. 狂暴地, 失控地
vocabulary.context: And it gets wilder - evolutionary biologist Rick Shine pits meat ants against cane toads.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事情变得更加疯狂--进化生物学家 Rick Shine 将肉蚁与甘蔗蟾蜍进行比较

vocabulary.definition: adv. 暂时地, 立刻, 随时地
vocabulary.context: The predators got momentarily sick - kind of like food poisoning - but built up immunity to the toad's toxin and learned to avoid eating adult toads which are the most powerful poisoners.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 捕食者暂时生病了--有点像食物中毒--但增强了对蟾蜍毒素的免疫力，并学会了避免吃成年蟾蜍，这是最强大的毒物

vocabulary.definition: n. 常在野外活动的人
vocabulary.context: Marin named this spotted cat "El Bonito" or "The Beautiful." Earlier in 2011, an outdoorsman and his ten-year-old daughter found another jaguar that their dog chased up a tree.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 马林将这只斑点猫命名为"El Bonito"或"美丽的"。 2011 年初，一位户外爱好者和他 10 岁的女儿发现了另一只美洲虎，被他们的狗追上了树

vocabulary.definition: n. 犰狳；[医] 南美犰狳
vocabulary.context: For example, jaguars eat tasty armadillos.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，美洲虎吃美味的犰狳

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 飞涨, 突升；n. 焰火
vocabulary.context: Without the jaguar, the number of armadillos would skyrocket.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果没有美洲虎，犰狳的数量将会猛增

vocabulary.definition: n. 蚯蚓
vocabulary.context: Armadillos eat earthworms, so with more armadillos, there would be fewer earthworms.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 犰狳吃蚯蚓，所以犰狳越多，蚯蚓就会越少

vocabulary.definition: n. 分裂, 破碎；[计] 在IP层将打包文件切成适当大小的程序
vocabulary.context: In addition, the destruction of habitat has resulted in population fragmentation.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 此外，栖息地的破坏导致了种群的分裂

vocabulary.definition: n. 通道, 走廊
vocabulary.context: Conservationists are working to create a "jaguar corridor," or a passageway for jaguars across 18 Latin American countries.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 自然资源保护主义者正在努力创建一条"美洲虎走廊"，即横跨 18 个拉丁美洲国家的美洲虎通道

vocabulary.definition: n. 走廊, 回廊, 人口密集地带
vocabulary.context: Conservationists are working to create a "jaguar corridor," or a passageway for jaguars across 18 Latin American countries.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 自然资源保护主义者正在努力创建一条"美洲虎走廊"，即横跨 18 个拉丁美洲国家的美洲虎通道

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 繁荣, 茂盛, 活跃, 手舞足蹈；v.t. 挥舞, 夸耀；n. 挥舞, 炫耀, 茂盛, 兴旺, 华丽词藻
vocabulary.context: That might not sound so bad, but earthworms maintain the soil, allowing plants to grow and flourish.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这听起来可能没那么糟糕，但蚯蚓可以维持土壤，让植物生长茂盛

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不宜于呼吸的,不宜吸入的
vocabulary.context: Imagine being wrapped in a heavy, unbreathable blanket.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 想象一下被裹在一条厚重、不透气的毯子里

vocabulary.definition: (white-wash 的过去时) n. 白涂料, 粉刷用石灰水, 香粉, 油脂, 雪花膏, 粉饰, 掩盖真相, 掩饰, 美化；v.t. 刷石灰水于..., 涂白, 粉刷, 粉饰, 美化, 掩盖, 掩饰, 彻底击败(对手)
vocabulary.context: But the reef's breathtaking beauty is being white-washed.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但珊瑚礁令人惊叹的美丽正在被粉饰

vocabulary.definition: adv. 过早地, 贸然地
vocabulary.context: Collapsing dens leads to pups being prematurely separated from their mothers.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 巢穴倒塌导致幼崽过早地与母亲分离

vocabulary.definition: n. 海星
vocabulary.context: Fish feast on them, starfish use powerful arms to open their shells and enjoy the tender innards, birds pick up shellfish and crack open their shells.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 鱼儿以它们为食，海星用有力的手臂打开贝壳，享受嫩嫩的内脏，鸟类拿起贝类并敲开贝壳

vocabulary.definition: adj. 惊人的, 惊险的
vocabulary.context: But the reef's breathtaking beauty is being white-washed.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但珊瑚礁令人惊叹的美丽正在被粉饰

vocabulary.definition: n. 外部, 表面, 外貌；adj. 外部的, 外在的, 表面的
vocabulary.context: For example, the bright yellow butterflyfish eats the coral's mucus-like exterior layer.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，亮黄色蝴蝶鱼吃珊瑚的粘液状外层

vocabulary.definition: adj. 深远的, 广泛的, 伸至远处的
vocabulary.context: Warming temperatures have far-reaching impacts: oceans warm, glaciers melt, sea levels rise, and even the chemistry of the ocean changes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 气温变暖具有深远的影响：海洋变暖、冰川融化、海平面上升，甚至海洋的化学成分也发生变化

vocabulary.definition: n. 过度, 剩于, 超过, 超额；adj. 过量的, 额外的
vocabulary.context: Each day, the ocean absorbs 22 million tons of excess carbon dioxide made from burning fossil fuels according to Smithsonian .
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 根据史密森学会的数据，海洋每天吸收 2200 万吨因燃烧化石燃料而产生的过量二氧化碳

vocabulary.definition: n. 寄宿处
vocabulary.context: Her parents, Elizabeth and Thomas Jennings, ran a boardinghouse.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她的父母伊丽莎白·詹宁斯和托马斯·詹宁斯经营着一家寄宿公寓

vocabulary.definition: n. 马蹄铁；v.t. 装蹄铁于
vocabulary.context: The Third Avenue trolley rounded the New York City corner with a clatter of horseshoes on cobblestones.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 第三大道电车绕过纽约市的拐角处，发出马蹄铁踩在鹅卵石上的咔哒声

vocabulary.definition: adj. （用作定语）用马拖的
vocabulary.context: By the 1850's New York City's 683 horse-drawn trolleys were the fastest way to get around town.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 到 1850 年代，纽约市的 683 辆马车是游览城镇最快的交通工具

vocabulary.definition: adj. 禁止进入的, 禁止使用的；[化] 超出范围的
vocabulary.context: Also, the best schools and best jobs were off-limits to Blacks, as were most of the restaurants, theaters, and concert halls that Elizabeth passed on her regular trips around town.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 此外，最好的学校和最好的工作都禁止黑人进入，伊丽莎白在城里定期旅行时经过的大多数餐馆、剧院和音乐厅也是如此

vocabulary.definition: adj. 苦干的, 不辞辛劳的
vocabulary.context: Born in New York City in 1830, Elizabeth Jennings grew up in a hardworking family.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 伊丽莎白·詹宁斯 1830 年出生于纽约市，在一个勤劳的家庭中长大

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 容纳过多的人, 使过度拥挤
vocabulary.context: Instead, people like Elizabeth had to wait for separate, overcrowded "Colored" cars.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 相反，像伊丽莎白这样的人不得不等待单独的、过度拥挤的"有色人种"汽车

vocabulary.definition: adj. 打昏迷的, 弄得人昏头昏脑的, 令人震惊的
vocabulary.context: In a stunning decision, they found the Third Avenue Railroad Company guilty of all charges.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在一项令人震惊的决定中，他们裁定第三大道铁路公司所有指控均成立

vocabulary.definition: adj. 值得尊重的, 人格高尚的, 相当数量的；n. 品格高尚的人
vocabulary.context: In court, he pointed out a new state law, which said that trolley companies were "common carriers." As such, they were required to carry all respectable persons, no matter what their race.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在法庭上，他指出了一项新的州法律，其中规定电车公司是"公共承运人"。因此，他们必须搭载所有受人尊敬的人，无论他们的种族如何

vocabulary.definition: adj. 防弹的
vocabulary.context: He drew some pictures of purely imaginary fish, such as the Buffalo Carp Sucker and the Devil Jack-Diamond Fish, which he described as 10 feet long, with bulletproof scales.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他画了一些纯粹想象中的鱼的图片，比如水牛鲤鱼和魔鬼杰克钻石鱼，他描述这些鱼有 10 英尺长，有防弹鳞片

vocabulary.definition: v.t. / v.i. 医疗, 行医（doctor的过去式与过去分词形式）
vocabulary.context: The website is meant to show how easy it is to make fake information seem real, with doctored photos and official-sounding (but made-up) science facts.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 该网站旨在展示通过篡改照片和听起来官方（但捏造的）科学事实来使虚假信息看起来真实是多么容易

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 欺骗, 哄骗, 愚弄；n. 愚弄人, 恶作剧
vocabulary.context: These famous hoaxes fooled many people. When something sounds too good - or weird - to be true... maybe it isn't. It always pays to check your facts.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些著名的骗局愚弄了很多人。当某件事听起来太好--或者太奇怪--令人难以置信时......也许事实并非如此。检查你的事实总是值得的

vocabulary.definition: n. 缓冲器, 满杯；adj. 大胜利的
vocabulary.context: On April Fools' Day in 1957, a British television news show reported that Swiss farmers were enjoying a bumper crop of spaghetti, thanks to the elimination of that dreaded pest, the spaghetti weevil.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1957年愚人节，英国电视新闻节目报道称，由于消灭了可怕的害虫--意大利面象鼻虫，瑞士农民迎来了意大利面大丰收

vocabulary.definition: n. 章鱼
vocabulary.context: Save the Pacific Tree Octopus!
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 拯救太平洋树章鱼！

vocabulary.definition: n. 乳儿, 吸管；[医] 吸盘
vocabulary.context: He drew some pictures of purely imaginary fish, such as the Buffalo Carp Sucker and the Devil Jack-Diamond Fish, which he described as 10 feet long, with bulletproof scales.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他画了一些纯粹想象中的鱼的图片，比如水牛鲤鱼和魔鬼杰克钻石鱼，他描述这些鱼有 10 英尺长，有防弹鳞片

vocabulary.definition: n. 英尺长度, 英板尺, (影片的)连续镜头
vocabulary.context: Film footage showed farmers pulling limp noodles from the branches of trees.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 电影片段显示农民从树枝上拉出松软的面条

vocabulary.definition: n. 网站（全球资讯网的主机站）
vocabulary.context: According to the website zapatopi.net/treeoctopus , tree octopuses are real, endangered, and need saving.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 根据网站 zapatopi.net/treeoctopus，树章鱼是真实存在的，濒临灭绝，需要拯救

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 露出, 显示, 透露, 揭露, 泄露, (神)启示；n. 窗侧, 门侧
vocabulary.context: Then Audubon revealed the fakes and had the last laugh.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然后奥杜邦揭露了假货，笑到了最后

vocabulary.definition: n. 有声电影
vocabulary.context: The first "talkie," the 1927 movie The Jazz Singer , was actually mostly silent.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 第一部"有声电影"是 1927 年的电影《爵士歌手》，实际上大部分时间都是无声的

vocabulary.definition: n. 表演娱乐节目的人, 演艺人员
vocabulary.context: But it did feature some words and singing by the popular entertainer Al Jolson.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但它确实有一些流行艺人 Al Jolson 的歌词和演唱

vocabulary.definition: n. 爱好, 嗜好, 由衷的高兴
vocabulary.context: Americans embraced them with gusto.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 美国人热情地拥抱它们

vocabulary.definition: n. 首长, 最高当局；adj. 最重要的, 最高的, 至上的
vocabulary.context: The Big Five included Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer (MGM), Paramount Pictures, RKO Pictures, Warner Brothers, and 20th Century Fox.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 五巨头包括米高梅 (MGM)、派拉蒙影业、雷电华影业、华纳兄弟和二十世纪福克斯

vocabulary.definition: adj. 轻佻的, 妄动的, 琐碎的；[法] 轻薄的, 轻浮的, 无意义的
vocabulary.context: Many Americans' thoughts turned to more frivolous ideas.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 许多美国人的思想转向了更无聊的想法

vocabulary.definition: n. 汽船, 轮船
vocabulary.context: The following year, Walt Disney made the first talking animated film, Steamboat Willie .
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 次年，华特迪士尼制作了第一部有声动画电影《威利汽船》

vocabulary.definition: adj. 活生生的, 能活动的, 活泼的, 欢快的
vocabulary.context: The following year, Walt Disney made the first talking animated film, Steamboat Willie .
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 次年，华特迪士尼制作了第一部有声动画电影《威利汽船》

vocabulary.definition: n. 喜剧, 有趣的事情
vocabulary.context: Together, they listened to broadcasts ranging from comedy and variety shows to news, drama, and sporting events.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们一起收听从喜剧和综艺节目到新闻、戏剧和体育赛事的广播

vocabulary.definition: n. 来源, 水源, 根源, 原始资料, 源；[计] 来源, 源程序
vocabulary.context: The radio was Americans' first source of mechanized entertainment - and Americans enthusiastically embraced the invention.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 收音机是美国人第一个机械化娱乐来源--美国人热情地接受了这项发明

vocabulary.definition: adj. 掌上型；手持型
vocabulary.context: (Fakers still love to record "paranormal activity" with cell phones and handheld video recorders.) "Photographing" UFOs became popular after World War II.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: （Fakers仍然喜欢用手机和手持录像机记录"超自然活动"。）"拍摄"UFO在二战后开始流行

vocabulary.definition: n. 跳动;易变;神精质
vocabulary.context: If there's a pattern to the jumpiness, it's a fake.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果跳动有规律，那么它就是假货

vocabulary.definition: adj. 超过正常的, 超过科学可知范围的；[医] 轻度异常的
vocabulary.context: (Fakers still love to record "paranormal activity" with cell phones and handheld video recorders.) "Photographing" UFOs became popular after World War II.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: （Fakers仍然喜欢用手机和手持录像机记录"超自然活动"。）"拍摄"UFO在二战后开始流行

vocabulary.definition: adj. 常识的; 有生活经验得来的
vocabulary.context: Of course, there are also some simple common-sense ways to spot an imposter just by looking.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当然，还有一些简单的常识性方法可以通过观察来识别冒名顶替者

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未被认出的, 未经确认的
vocabulary.context: Falsified pictures of ghosts, unidentified flying objects (UFOs), and monsters have been around for years.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 鬼魂、不明飞行物 (UFO) 和怪物的伪造图片已经存在多年

vocabulary.definition: adj. 概略的, 草草完成的
vocabulary.context: Whoa - if you think those last three sound sketchy, you should.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 哇 - 如果你认为最后三个听起来很粗略，你应该这样做

vocabulary.definition: n. 三脚桌, 三脚架；[医] 三脚架, 三脚台
vocabulary.context: One of Allain's favorite techniques is to analyze camera shake - you know, the quaking that occurs in a video when the person filming it holds the camera in their hand instead of mounting it on a tripod.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 阿兰最喜欢的技术之一是分析相机抖动 - 你知道，当拍摄视频的人手持相机而不是将其安装在三脚架上时，视频中会发生震动

vocabulary.definition: n. 冒名顶替者, 骗子；[法] 冒名顶替者, 骗子
vocabulary.context: Of course, there are also some simple common-sense ways to spot an imposter just by looking.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当然，还有一些简单的常识性方法可以通过观察来识别冒名顶替者

vocabulary.definition: n. 秘密, 保密；[法] 秘密, 秘密状态, 保密
vocabulary.context: Legions of animal trainers who were sworn to secrecy participated.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 大批宣誓保密的驯兽师参加了此次活动

vocabulary.definition: n. 无边便帽, 黄芩属植物；[医] 美黄芩
vocabulary.context: "They're also the best, bibi ," smiled Khan, a big, bearded man wearing a spotless white skullcap and kaftan while weighing a bunch of ripe bananas and a handful of golden apples.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "他们也是最好的，bibi，"Khan 笑着说，他是一个大块头，留着胡须，戴着一尘不染的白色无边便帽，穿着长袍，同时称着一串成熟的香蕉和一把金苹果

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 把…击碎,把…炸成碎片
vocabulary.context: As if on cue, the entire troop of monkeys fell upon the wallet and ransacked it, mercilessly tearing apart more bundles of cash, tossing some notes to the wind and ripping others to smithereens.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 仿佛在暗示一样，整群猴子都扑向钱包并洗劫了它，无情地撕碎了更多的现金，将一些钞票扔到了风中，并将其他钞票撕成碎片

vocabulary.definition: n. 圣所, 密室, 私室
vocabulary.context: While Mamma continued to pray, I silently backed away from Kali and circled the inner sanctum three times while fervently repeating under my breath, "I wish I get Saloni, I wish I get Saloni, I wish I get Saloni..."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当妈妈继续祈祷时，我默默地从卡利身边退开，绕着内殿转了三圈，同时低声热切地重复着："我希望我得到萨罗尼，我希望我得到萨罗尼，我希望我得到萨罗尼......"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 微温的, 温热的, 不太热烈的；[医] 微温的
vocabulary.context: The summer sun suddenly looked bleak, the sunshine tepid.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 夏日的阳光突然显得暗淡，阳光不温不火

vocabulary.definition: n. (=caftan)土耳其式长衫
vocabulary.context: "They're also the best, bibi ," smiled Khan, a big, bearded man wearing a spotless white skullcap and kaftan while weighing a bunch of ripe bananas and a handful of golden apples.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "他们也是最好的，bibi，"Khan 笑着说，他是一个大块头，留着胡须，戴着一尘不染的白色无边便帽，穿着长袍，同时称着一串成熟的香蕉和一把金苹果

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 进行战斗, 作战（war的过去式与过去分词形式）
vocabulary.context: When the demons warred with the gods to become masters of the universe, and the gods stood on the brink of defeat, they joined forces to give birth to the all-powerful Kali.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当恶魔与诸神争夺宇宙主宰的战争，诸神濒临失败时，他们联手诞生了无所不能的卡利

vocabulary.definition: n. (印度男女点在额头的)小红点
vocabulary.context: On my way out the priest placed a tiny red tilak on my forehead and offered me a generous helping of sticky-sweet prasad.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在我出去的路上，牧师在我的额头上放了一块小小的红色蒂拉克，并给了我一份粘稠的甜普拉萨德

vocabulary.definition: adv. 无礼地, 粗鲁地, 唐突地
vocabulary.context: He donned them and surveyed his surroundings with renewed interest before getting rid of them unceremoniously.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他穿上它们，以新的兴趣审视周围的环境，然后毫不客气地摆脱它们

vocabulary.definition: adj. 着火的, 闪亮的, 激昂的；adv. 着火, 闪耀
vocabulary.context: The dark-skinned, four-armed Kali - her eyes ablaze with fury and her red tongue hanging out - sent little shivers down my spine.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 深色皮肤、四臂的卡莉--她的眼睛里闪烁着愤怒的光芒，她伸出红色的舌头--让我的脊椎微微颤抖

vocabulary.definition: n. 礼拜者, 参拜者, 崇拜者( worshiper的复数形式 )
vocabulary.context: As we neared Kalibari, I heard the nonstop clang of temple bells as barefoot worshipers made their way in and out of the temple.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当我们接近卡利巴里时，我听到寺庙钟声不断，赤脚的信徒进出寺庙

vocabulary.definition: n. 一群
vocabulary.context: on bevies of barnacles
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 藤壶群

vocabulary.definition: n. 暗黄色, 暗黄色皮革, 人的皮肤；v.t. 擦亮, 使柔软；adj. 暗黄色的
vocabulary.context: buffed by the crush
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 被粉碎磨光了

vocabulary.definition: n. 北极雁, 藤壶
vocabulary.context: on bevies of barnacles
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 藤壶群

vocabulary.definition: n. 雀斑, 斑点；[医] 非曝露部雀斑
vocabulary.context: Her fair freckled face
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她那张白皙、有雀斑的脸

vocabulary.definition: n. 树皮, 吠声；v.i. 吠, 叫骂；v.t. 喊出, 剥树皮
vocabulary.context: she'd barked
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她叫了一声

vocabulary.definition: adj. 怪诞的, 可怕的, 奇异的, 令人迷惑不解的
vocabulary.context: An eerie unquiet
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 诡异的不安

vocabulary.definition: n. 失事, 残骸, 破坏；v.t. 使失事, 拆毁, 破坏；v.i. (船)失事, 营救失事船只
vocabulary.context: waking a wreck
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 唤醒沉船

vocabulary.definition: n. 压碎, 粉碎, 群众, 迷恋；v.t. 压破, 征服, 塞, 弄皱, 榨出；v.i. 被压碎, 起皱, 挤
vocabulary.context: buffed by the crush
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 被粉碎磨光了

vocabulary.definition: n. 财富, 资源, 财产, 丰富, 富裕, 大量；[经] 财富
vocabulary.context: it held wealth
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它拥有财富

vocabulary.definition: n. [生化]支链淀粉,胶淀粉
vocabulary.context: One part of starch is a molecule called amylopectin.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 淀粉的一部分是一种称为支链淀粉的分子

vocabulary.definition: n. 泄气, 腐败, 陈腐
vocabulary.context: But what causes staleness isn't water loss.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但导致陈旧的原因并不是水分流失

vocabulary.definition: adj. 容易破裂的, 清脆的
vocabulary.context: The dough hardens and becomes crunchy and crackly.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 面团变硬，变得松脆易裂

vocabulary.definition: n. 变压器, 促使变化的人；[化] 变压器
vocabulary.context: But it's also an amazing transformer.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但它也是一个令人惊叹的变压器

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 揉, 按摩, 捏；[机] 揉合
vocabulary.context: As you knead it-stretch it, roll it, and flatten it-the blob soon becomes smooth.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当你揉捏它时--拉伸它、滚动它、压平它--斑点很快就会变得光滑

vocabulary.definition: n. 烤面包器, 烤面包炉, 祝酒人
vocabulary.context: To make stale bread more appealing, it's best to put it in the oven or toaster.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 为了使不新鲜的面包更有吸引力，最好将其放入烤箱或烤面包机中

vocabulary.definition: n. 比萨饼
vocabulary.context: "The classic example is to think about pizza dough," says Andrew Ross, a scientist at Oregon State University.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "典型的例子是披萨面团，"俄勒冈州立大学的科学家安德鲁·罗斯说

vocabulary.definition: n. 变形, 变质, 变化, 改革, 变换；[化] 转化; 变换
vocabulary.context: "Baking is one of the most fascinating transformations in cooking," Ross says.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "烘焙是烹饪中最迷人的转变之一，"罗斯说

vocabulary.definition: n. [植]柳枝稷
vocabulary.context: "Could we use corn or switchgrass as an alternative to trees?" he asks.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "我们可以用玉米或柳枝稷来代替树木吗？"他问道

vocabulary.definition: n. 木质素；[化] 木质素; 木素
vocabulary.context: Lignin is a natural polymer.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 木质素是一种天然聚合物

vocabulary.definition: adj. 油基的
vocabulary.context: They designed these chains to mimic the oil-based types used for today's tape adhesives.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们设计这些链条是为了模仿当今胶带粘合剂所使用的油基类型

vocabulary.definition: adj. 粘着的, 涂有粘性物质的；n. 粘合剂, 橡皮膏
vocabulary.context: Sticky tape has been around for more than 80 years. It relies on a special kind of glue. As you push down on a piece of tape, the adhesive on the sticky side seals tightly. But you can easily peel it back off. Some adhesives even come off without leaving anything behind.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 胶带已经存在了 80 多年。它依赖于一种特殊的胶水。当您按下胶带时，粘性一侧的粘合剂会紧密密封。但您可以轻松地将其剥离。有些粘合剂甚至脱落后没有留下任何东西

vocabulary.definition: n. 碳氢化合物；[化] 烃; 碳氢化合物
vocabulary.context: Companies today use the hydrocarbons that make up this oil for their tape adhesives.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如今，许多公司使用这种油中的碳氢化合物来制造胶带粘合剂

vocabulary.definition: n. 化学家, 药剂师；[化] 化学家; 化学师; 化学工作者; 药剂师; 药房
vocabulary.context: A chemist at the University of Groningen in the Netherlands, he did not take part in the new research.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 荷兰格罗宁根大学化学家，他没有参与这项新研究

vocabulary.definition: n. 底侧；下降趋势
vocabulary.context: But tape has a downside.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但胶带也有缺点

vocabulary.definition: n. 浸, 湿透, 大雨；v.t. 使上下湿透, 浸, 吸入, 吸收, 浸洗掉；v.i. 浸泡, 渗透
vocabulary.context: They soaked their wood in chemicals to break the lignin's long, chain-like molecules into smaller bits.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们将木材浸泡在化学品中，将木质素的长链状分子分解成更小的碎片

vocabulary.definition: adj. 通常的, 共同的, 通俗的, 公共的；[计] 公用块
vocabulary.context: One common source is crude oil.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一种常见的来源是原油

vocabulary.definition: num. 第十亿个, 十亿分之一
vocabulary.context: It can display the surface of materials to a resolution of 1 nanometer (a billionth of a meter).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它可以以 1 纳米（十亿分之一米）的分辨率显示材料表面

vocabulary.definition: n. 氟；[化] 氟
vocabulary.context: These are molecules made from a mix of fluorine, hydrogen and carbon.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些分子由氟、氢和碳的混合物组成

vocabulary.definition: adj. slippery（滑的）的最高级
vocabulary.context: For a two-minute downhill race, skiers want the slipperiest surface possible.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对于两分钟的速降比赛，滑雪者需要尽可能最滑的路面

vocabulary.definition: n. 滑雪的人
vocabulary.context: When the world's top skiers face off this week at the Winter Olympics, they will be relying on years of training, mental preparation - and a good deal of chemistry.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当世界顶级滑雪运动员本周在冬季奥运会上对决时，他们将依靠多年的训练、心理准备以及大量的化学反应

vocabulary.definition: n. 丙酮；[化] 丙酮
vocabulary.context: Some fluorocarbon ski waxes also have extra hydrocarbons, such as acetone (ASS-eh-toan), benzene or toluene (TAAL-yu-een).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一些氟碳滑雪蜡还含有额外的碳氢化合物，例如丙酮（ASS-eh-toan）、苯或甲苯（TAAL-yu-een）

vocabulary.definition: adj. 新降落下的; 刚落下的
vocabulary.context: Randall remembers her skis picking up new-fallen snow in Finland earlier this season.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 兰德尔记得她的滑雪板在本赛季早些时候在芬兰拾起了新雪

vocabulary.definition: n. 甲苯；[化] 甲苯
vocabulary.context: Some fluorocarbon ski waxes also have extra hydrocarbons, such as acetone (ASS-eh-toan), benzene or toluene (TAAL-yu-een).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一些氟碳滑雪蜡还含有额外的碳氢化合物，例如丙酮（ASS-eh-toan）、苯或甲苯（TAAL-yu-een）

vocabulary.definition: adj. 添加的, 附加的, 加法的, 累积的；n. 添加物
vocabulary.context: (These chemicals are related to additives that manufacturers sometimes use to make rain gear repel water.)
vocabulary.contextTranslation: （这些化学物质与制造商有时用来制造雨具防水的添加剂有关。）

vocabulary.definition: n. (滑雪板上的)湿雪蜡
vocabulary.context: Sometimes they add a sticky wax called "klister" - or kick wax - to the part of the ski below the boot.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 有时，他们会在滑雪靴下方的滑雪板部分添加一种称为"klister"（或踢蜡）的粘性蜡

vocabulary.definition: adv. 用化学, 以化学方法
vocabulary.context: When exposed to sunlight, this coating chemically bonds to the ski.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当暴露在阳光下时，这种涂层会与滑雪板发生化学结合

vocabulary.definition: n. 稷, 粟, 黍；[医] 小米
vocabulary.context: She wakes up at 4:00 a.m., eats millet porridge, and then walks two miles to her family's cocoa bean field.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她凌晨 4:00 起床，吃小米粥，然后步行两英里到她家的可可豆田

vocabulary.definition: n. 粥, 糊
vocabulary.context: She wakes up at 4:00 a.m., eats millet porridge, and then walks two miles to her family's cocoa bean field.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她凌晨 4:00 起床，吃小米粥，然后步行两英里到她家的可可豆田

vocabulary.definition: n. 可可粉, 可可茶, 可可色；[医] 可可, 可可豆
vocabulary.context: But many people don't know that children in West Africa pick most of the world's cocoa beans.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但很多人不知道，世界上大部分可可豆都是西非的儿童采摘的

vocabulary.definition: n. 成分, 因素；[化] 配合剂; 拼料; 成分; 组分
vocabulary.context: (Cocoa is the main ingredient in chocolate.) People who buy chocolate are becoming more and more worried about child labor.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: （可可是巧克力的主要成分。）购买巧克力的人们越来越担心童工问题

vocabulary.definition: v. 证明, 保证
vocabulary.context: For example, a group of farms in Africa and South America are called Fair Trade Certified.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，非洲和南美洲的一组农场被称为公平贸易认证农场

vocabulary.definition: n. 象牙, 乳白色；adj. 象牙制的, 乳白色的
vocabulary.context: Ten-year-old Sametta lives in Cote d'Ivoire (or Ivory Coast), a country in West Africa.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 十岁的萨梅塔住在西非国家科特迪瓦（或象牙海岸）

vocabulary.definition: n. 野生动植物
vocabulary.context: The Rainforest Alliance is also working to improve life for farmers, teaching them ways to protect soils, waterways, and wildlife while increasing their yields, ensuring that their children go to school and eliminating child labor.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 雨林联盟还致力于改善农民的生活，教他们保护土壤、水道和野生动物的方法，同时提高产量，确保他们的孩子上学并消除童工

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 除去, 排除, 剔除, 消除
