vocabulary.definition: n. 大剪刀；[电] 截割器
vocabulary.context: Tea clippers were fast ships.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 茶叶快船是快船

vocabulary.definition: n. 桅, 桅杆, 杆；v.t. 装桅杆于
vocabulary.context: Two tall masts are tilted slightly toward the back of the boat.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 两根高桅杆略微向船后倾斜

vocabulary.definition: n. 海豚, 鼠海豚
vocabulary.context: Porpoises often swim alongside the ship.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 海豚经常在船边游泳

vocabulary.definition: adv. 巨大地, 非常地
vocabulary.context: "That can be a hugely wonderful experience."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "这可能是一次非常美妙的经历。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 海盗, 盗印者, 侵犯专利权者；v.t. 盗印, 掠夺, 翻印；v.i. 做海盗, 从事劫掠
vocabulary.context: On the Pride of Baltimore II , you don't just pretend to sail a ship like ones that pirates sailed long ago.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在《巴尔的摩之傲 II》中，你不只是假装驾驶一艘像很久以前海盗航行的船一样

vocabulary.definition: n. 航行, 航海, 导航；[经] 航空, 航海, 航船
vocabulary.context: The captain or mates give lessons on navigation and steering.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 船长或大副教授导航和操舵课程

vocabulary.definition: n. 配偶, 对手, 助手, (象棋)将死；v.t. 使配对, 使一致, 结伴, (象棋)将死；v.i. 成配偶, 紧密配合
vocabulary.context: The captain or mates give lessons on navigation and steering.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 船长或大副教授导航和操舵课程

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 鬼鬼祟祟做事；v.t. 偷偷地做；n. 鬼鬼祟祟的人, 偷偷摸摸的行为, 帆布胶底运动鞋；adj. 暗中进行的
vocabulary.context: They were often used to sneak up on larger ships and steal their cargo.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们经常被用来偷偷潜入较大的船只并窃取其货物

vocabulary.definition: n. 铅, 铅条, 领导, 超前量, 领引, 榜样, 主角, 导线；v.t. 引导, 带领, 领导, 指挥, 致使, 加铅于, 用铅包；v.i. 领导, 带头, 导致, 用测深锤测深, 被铅覆盖；adj. 带头的, 最重要的
vocabulary.context: If you're lucky - and daring - you might also get a chance to climb the rope ladders leading to the very tops of the sails.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果您足够幸运且足够大胆，您还可能有机会爬上通往帆顶的绳梯

vocabulary.definition: adj. 为时一周的,持续一星期的
vocabulary.context: After a nearly weeklong siege, Chief Joseph ended his people's flight to freedom.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 经过近一周的围困，约瑟夫酋长结束了他的人民逃往自由的旅程

vocabulary.definition: n. 加拿大盘羊
vocabulary.context: It is more famously known as the Battle of the Little Bighorn.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 更为著名的是小大角羊之战

vocabulary.definition: adv. 无用；失败地
vocabulary.context: As a member of a nonsigning group, Chief Joseph tried unsuccessfully to negotiate for his people to keep their homeland.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 作为非签名团体的成员，约瑟夫酋长试图为他的人民保住家园进行谈判，但没有成功

vocabulary.definition: adj. 辛酸的, 尖锐的, 剧烈的
vocabulary.context: Chief Joseph is credited with giving a poignant speech on October 5, 1877.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 约瑟夫酋长因在 1877 年 10 月 5 日发表了一次令人心酸的演讲而受到赞誉

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 为...报复, 报仇
vocabulary.context: While they camped, several young men sought to avenge a murder committed by a white man the year before.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当他们露营时，几名年轻人试图为一名白人一年前犯下的谋杀案报仇

vocabulary.definition: n. 口才, 雄辩术, 雄辩；[法] 善辩, 口才
vocabulary.context: He spoke for his people with eloquence and dignity.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他雄辩而有尊严地为他的人民说话

vocabulary.definition: adj. 衷心的, 真心真意的
vocabulary.context: His simple but heartfelt words captured the situation faced by all Native Americans when dealing with the U.S.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他简单而真挚的话语道出了所有美洲原住民在与美国打交道时所面临的处境

vocabulary.definition: n. 发言人, 代言者
vocabulary.context: He was the leader and spokesman of one band of the Nez Perce.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他是内兹珀斯人一支部落的领袖和代言人

vocabulary.definition: adv. 不知疲倦地；不屈不挠地
vocabulary.context: He and his friend John Woolman worked tirelessly to change people's minds.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他和他的朋友约翰·伍尔曼孜孜不倦地努力改变人们的想法

vocabulary.definition: adv. 错误地, 不恰当地, 不正确地, 不正直地, 不公正地
vocabulary.context: The hundreds of thousands of Black people who were enslaved knew they were being treated wrongly.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 数十万被奴役的黑人知道他们受到了错误的对待

vocabulary.definition: n. 小册子, 活页文选
vocabulary.context: Anthony wrote pamphlets and books that explained why slavery was unjust.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 安东尼写了小册子和书籍来解释为什么奴隶制是不公正的

vocabulary.definition: n. 废除主义者, 废奴主义者
vocabulary.context: Anthony became one of the earliest abolitionists , people who believed in ending slavery.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 安东尼成为最早的废奴主义者之一，他们相信结束奴隶制

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不公平的；[法] 不公正的人; 不公正的, 不公平的, 不合理的
vocabulary.context: Anthony wrote pamphlets and books that explained why slavery was unjust.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 安东尼写了小册子和书籍来解释为什么奴隶制是不公正的

vocabulary.definition: adj. 天资高的, 有才能的
vocabulary.context: Anthony told people that Black students were just as talented as white students.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 安东尼告诉人们，黑人学生和白人学生一样有才华

vocabulary.definition: n. 天才, 天赋, 精神, 神魔；[医] 特征
vocabulary.context: He wrote of the "genius" he saw in his Black students.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他写到了他在黑人学生中看到的"天才"

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 不一致, 不适宜；[法] 抵触, 不同意, 争执
vocabulary.context: Anthony disagreed with this.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 安东尼不同意这一点

vocabulary.definition: n. 信念, 相信, 信仰
vocabulary.context: In 1758, Quaker groups in Pennsylvania and New Jersey announced that slavery was against Christian beliefs.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1758年，宾夕法尼亚州和新泽西州的贵格会团体宣布奴隶制违反基督教信仰

vocabulary.definition: n. 划艇
vocabulary.context: She was trying to escape a Union gunboat when the rowboat she was in overturned.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当她乘坐的划艇翻倒时，她正试图逃离一艘联邦炮舰

vocabulary.definition: n. 炮舰, 炮艇
vocabulary.context: She was trying to escape a Union gunboat when the rowboat she was in overturned.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当她乘坐的划艇翻倒时，她正试图逃离一艘联邦炮舰

vocabulary.definition: n. 美女
vocabulary.context: Belle Boyd was 18 years old when Union troops invaded her hometown in western Virginia.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当联邦军队入侵贝尔·博伊德位于弗吉尼亚州西部的家乡时，她才 18 岁

vocabulary.definition: n. 零, 暗号, 密码, 无用的东西, 阿拉伯数字；v. 计算, 做算术
vocabulary.context: Van Lew also created a cipher to safeguard the facts in her communications.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: Van Lew 还创建了一个密码来保护她通信中的事实

vocabulary.definition: n. 衬裙, 裙子
vocabulary.context: Hoop skirts and petticoats made it easy to hide messages, papers, and small packages.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 箍裙和衬裙可以轻松隐藏信息、文件和小包裹

vocabulary.definition: n. 侦探；adj. 侦探的
vocabulary.context: Union secret detective Allan Pinkerton grew suspicious of Greenhow and eventually arrested her.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 联邦秘密侦探艾伦·平克顿对格林豪产生了怀疑，并最终逮捕了她

vocabulary.definition: n. 女演员
vocabulary.context: Actress Pauline Cushman was pro-Union, but she pretended to be a loyal Confederate.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 女演员宝琳·库什曼 (Pauline Cushman) 支持联邦，但她假装自己是忠诚的南方邦联成员

vocabulary.definition: n. 忠贞, 忠诚, 忠实；[法] 忠诚, 忠心
vocabulary.context: She was released after she took a loyalty oath.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她宣誓效忠后被释放

vocabulary.definition: n. 接骨木属植物
vocabulary.context: She grows vegetables and elderberries on her farm.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她在自己的农场种植蔬菜和接骨木浆果

vocabulary.definition: n. 维持, 维修费；[经] 维修费, 养护费, 保养
vocabulary.context: Through the season, we also do farm maintenance and upkeep projects."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在整个季节，我们还进行农场维护和保养项目。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 草堆, 皱眉, 怪脸；v.t. 割草；v.i. 割草, 做怪脸
vocabulary.context: June is more field work: planting, weeding, mowing, irrigation.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 六月是更多的田间工作：播种、除草、割草、灌溉

vocabulary.definition: n. 雪鞋；v.i. 穿雪鞋走路
vocabulary.context: Farms that are in cold climates may have skiing and snowshoe trails.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 气候寒冷的农场可能设有滑雪道和雪鞋步道

vocabulary.definition: n. 灌溉, 冲洗；[医] 冲洗法, 灌溉
vocabulary.context: June is more field work: planting, weeding, mowing, irrigation.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 六月是更多的田间工作：播种、除草、割草、灌溉

vocabulary.definition: adj. 器官的, 有机的, 组织的, 根本的；[医] 器官的, 有生命的, 有机的, 器质的
vocabulary.context: WWOOF stands for World Wide Organization of Organic Farmers.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: WWOOF 代表世界有机农民组织

vocabulary.definition: n. 维护, 保持, 维修, 生活费用, 坚持, 扶养；[计] 维护; 维修
vocabulary.context: Through the season, we also do farm maintenance and upkeep projects."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在整个季节，我们还进行农场维护和保养项目。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 修理, 补救, 修复；v.t. 修理, 修补, 补救, 恢复, 补偿；v.i. 修理, 修补, 补救, 恢复, 去, 常去, 集合
vocabulary.context: In the winter, the farmers need help repairing anything that was broken during the warm months.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在冬天，农民需要帮助修复在温暖的月份里损坏的任何东西

vocabulary.definition: n. 农场, 农田；v.t. 耕种；v.i. 种田
vocabulary.context: You can try farming for yourself!
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 你可以尝试自己种田！

vocabulary.definition: n. 女牛仔
vocabulary.context: Dude ranches have helped guests experience the cowboy and cowgirl life for more than 100 years!
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 花花公子牧场一百多年来一直帮助客人体验牛仔和女牛仔的生活！

vocabulary.definition: n. <美国、加拿大>纨绔子弟，花花公子； <俚>男人，家伙；v.t. <俚>打扮得讲究或花哨
vocabulary.context: People who lived or worked on ranches used to call people from the city "dudes." Dude ranches were invented to show people from cities what life tending to cattle was like.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在牧场生活或工作的人们过去常常称城市里的人为"家伙"。杜德牧场的发明是为了向城市里的人们展示养牛的生活是什么样的

vocabulary.definition: n. 种马
vocabulary.context: At White Stallion Ranch, a dude ranch in Arizona, team cattle sorting becomes a competition.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在亚利桑那州的白种马牧场（White Stallion Ranch），团队牛分拣变成了一场竞赛

vocabulary.definition: n. 风景, 景色, 舞台布景
vocabulary.context: Hiking is a fun way to see gorgeous scenery.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 徒步旅行是欣赏美丽风景的有趣方式

vocabulary.definition: n. 大农场经营者, 牧场工人
vocabulary.context: Real ranchers have to sort cattle.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 真正的牧场主必须对牛进行分类

vocabulary.definition: n. 大牧场；v. 经营牧场
vocabulary.context: Dude ranches have helped guests experience the cowboy and cowgirl life for more than 100 years!
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 花花公子牧场一百多年来一直帮助客人体验牛仔和女牛仔的生活！

vocabulary.definition: n. 徒步旅行, 远足, 涨价, 提高；v.i. 步行, 徒步旅行, 上升；v.t. 使高涨, 拉起
vocabulary.context: Hiking is a fun way to see gorgeous scenery.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 徒步旅行是欣赏美丽风景的有趣方式

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 走向, 有某种的倾向, 易于, 照顾, 注意；v.t. 照料, 护理
vocabulary.context: Cowboys and cowgirls tended to herds from the southern part of the U.S.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 牛仔和女牛仔照料来自美国南部的牛群

vocabulary.definition: n. 环境, 外界, 围绕；[计] 环境
vocabulary.context: and the beautiful environment you're in."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 以及你所处的美丽环境。"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 挑衅的; 对抗的
vocabulary.context: When the civil rights movement became more confrontational, King grew as a leader.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当民权运动变得更具对抗性时，金成长为一名领导者

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未受教育的, 无知的
vocabulary.context: Thousands of people - Black and white, and young and old, educated and uneducated - responded to his call.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 成千上万的人--黑人和白人、年轻人和老年人、受过教育的和未受过教育的人--响应了他的号召

vocabulary.definition: n. 演说者, 演讲者, 雄辩家, 原告, 请愿人；[法] 请愿人, 原告, 雄辩者
vocabulary.context: King stood out for his brilliance as an orator.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 金因其演说家的才华而脱颖而出

vocabulary.definition: n. 墩座, 矮隔墙, 指挥台；[医] 足, 吸足
vocabulary.context: Then, King came to the podium.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 随后，金来到讲台上

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 宣传
vocabulary.context: The MIA was formed to publicize and raise money to support the bus boycott.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: MIA 的成立是为了宣传和筹集资金支持抵制公交车活动

vocabulary.definition: n. 烽火, 灯塔；v. 照亮
vocabulary.context: They looked to him as a beacon of hope and inspiration.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们将他视为希望和灵感的灯塔

vocabulary.definition: n. 得体, 礼貌, 正派；[法] 正当, 正派, 合乎礼仪
vocabulary.context: He also believed that Americans had a basic sense of decency.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他还认为美国人有基本的正派意识

vocabulary.definition: n. 进步, 改善, 利用；[医] 改善, 好转, 进步
vocabulary.context: King had been reluctant at first to accept election as president of the Montgomery Improvement Association (MIA).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 金起初不愿意接受蒙哥马利进步协会（MIA）主席的选举

vocabulary.definition: adj. 男女合校的
vocabulary.context: In 1837, Oberlin College in Oberlin, Ohio, had become the first coeducational college in the United States.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1837年，俄亥俄州奥柏林的奥柏林学院成为美国第一所男女同校的学院

vocabulary.definition: adj. 独断独行的
vocabulary.context: But having had their first real taste of what it was like to be self-reliant and treated equally, women were determined to keep the momentum of their changing role in society.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但是，在第一次真正体验到自力更生和平等对待的滋味后，女性决心保持她们在社会中角色变化的势头

vocabulary.definition: abbr. Char Oil Energy Development Systems 焦油能源开发系统
vocabulary.context: Like flappers, college coeds exerted an air of unprecedented independence.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 就像时髦女郎一样，大学女生表现出前所未有的独立气质

vocabulary.definition: n. 打字员
vocabulary.context: They found jobs in stores and offices as clerks and typists.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们在商店和办公室找到了文员和打字员的工作

vocabulary.definition: n. 年长女伴；v.t. 伴护
vocabulary.context: They had behaved modestly and did not go out in public without a chaperone.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们举止谦虚，没有陪同人员的情况下不会外出

vocabulary.definition: n. 拍击者, 惊鸟器, 苍蝇拍, 拍击物, (英)未入社交界的少女
vocabulary.context: A flapper was an independent woman in her 20s who demanded equality and who showed disdain for "acceptable" women's behavior.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 摩登女郎是指20多岁的独立女性，她们要求平等，并对"可接受的"女性行为表现出蔑视

vocabulary.definition: adv. 金融上；财政上
vocabulary.context: As women entered the workforce, they earned salaries that allowed them to grow financially independent.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 随着女性进入劳动力市场，她们获得的薪水使她们能够在经济上独立

vocabulary.definition: adv. 适当地；谨慎地
vocabulary.context: They had behaved modestly and did not go out in public without a chaperone.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们举止谦虚，没有陪同人员的情况下不会外出

vocabulary.definition: n. 蔑视；v.t. 蔑视, 鄙弃
vocabulary.context: A flapper was an independent woman in her 20s who demanded equality and who showed disdain for "acceptable" women's behavior.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 摩登女郎是指20多岁的独立女性，她们要求平等，并对"可接受的"女性行为表现出蔑视

vocabulary.definition: n. 火力
vocabulary.context: Even the Indigenous Americans' greater numbers could not make up for the Europeans' firepower.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 即使美洲原住民数量较多也无法弥补欧洲人的火力

vocabulary.definition: n. 武器, 军备, 武器设计研制学
vocabulary.context: And, the Europeans' advanced weaponry gave them an enormous edge in fulfilling these desires.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 而且，欧洲人先进的武器装备使他们在实现这些愿望方面具有巨大的优势

vocabulary.definition: n. 立足处, 据点, 根据地
vocabulary.context: Little by little, the Europeans were gaining a foothold in the Southeast.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 欧洲人逐渐在东南部站稳了脚跟

vocabulary.definition: n. 火器, 枪炮
vocabulary.context: The Europeans brought with them metal tools, firearms, and many other objects unknown in North America.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 欧洲人带来了金属工具、火器和许多其他北美未知的物品

vocabulary.definition: n. 贵重物品；[经] 贵重物品
vocabulary.context: Southeastern Indigenous Americans were eager to trade their own resources for these new valuables.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 美国东南部原住民渴望用自己的资源换取这些新的贵重物品

vocabulary.definition: n. 免疫, 免疫性, 免除；[化] 免疫性
vocabulary.context: Because they had never been exposed to these illnesses and had no built-in immunity, the diseases swept through the tribes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 由于他们从未接触过这些疾病，也没有内在的免疫力，所以疾病席卷了整个部落

vocabulary.definition: n. 东南, 东南地区；adj. 东南的, 向东南的, 来自东南的；adv. 往东南, 来自东南
vocabulary.context: This first encounter between Europeans and Indigenous Americans of the Southeast was far from peaceful.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 欧洲人和东南部美洲原住民之间的第一次相遇远非和平

vocabulary.definition: adj. 可疑的, 多疑的, 怀疑的；[法] 怀疑的, 令人怀疑的, 可疑的
vocabulary.context: There were Indigenous American tribes who were deeply suspicious of the Europeans, however.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，有些美洲原住民部落对欧洲人抱有深深的怀疑

vocabulary.definition: n. & adj.[动]蜥脚类动物( 的)
vocabulary.context: It was a sauropod, a type of plant-eating dinosaur that walked on all four legs and had a small head at the end of a really long neck, a massive body, and a long, whip-like tail.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它是一种蜥脚类恐龙，是一种食草恐龙，用四足行走，在很长的脖子末端有一个小头，巨大的身体和一条长长的鞭状尾巴

vocabulary.definition: n. 重量级拳击运动员, (非正式)有影响的要人；adj. 最重量级的
vocabulary.context: Meet Argentinosaurus , a super heavyweight that tipped the scales at somewhere between 66 to 100 tons - twice the weight of Supersaurus and about the same as an entire herd of 20 or so African elephants.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 认识一下阿根廷龙，它是一种超重量级动物，重量在 66 吨到 100 吨之间，是超级龙的两倍，大约相当于整个 20 头左右非洲象群的重量

vocabulary.definition: n. 矮小的人, 小人物, 无足轻重的东西
vocabulary.context: As they do, we'll learn more about these amazing creatures - both whoppers and pipsqueaks alike - and find new record breakers.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 正如他们所做的那样，我们将更多地了解这些令人惊叹的生物--无论是皇堡还是小怪--并找到新的纪录打破者

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有四根桅樯的
vocabulary.context: It's clear that sauropods, the long-necked, four-legged plant eaters, towered above other dinosaurs, including monstrous meat-eaters like Tyrannosaurus rex and Spinosaurus .
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 很明显，蜥脚类恐龙是一种长颈、四足的食草动物，其地位高于其他恐龙，包括像霸王龙和棘龙这样的巨型食肉动物

vocabulary.definition: adj. 发现者, 寻星镜；[医] 寻觅器, 探示器
vocabulary.context: Paleontologist Brian Curtice uncovered mistakes that a fossil finder had made 50 years ago when Supersaurus was first discovered.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 古生物学家布莱恩·柯蒂斯 (Brian Curtice) 发现了 50 年前首次发现超龙时化石发现者所犯的错误

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未命名的, 没有名字的, 未提及的；[计] 无名的
vocabulary.context: Then again, a new sauropod - still unnamed - that paleontologists began digging up in 2012 in Argentina looks like it was even larger.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 话又说回来，古生物学家于 2012 年在阿根廷挖掘出一种新的蜥脚类恐龙 - 仍未命名 - 看起来它更大

vocabulary.definition: n. 出版物, 出版, 公布；[经] 发布
vocabulary.context: As paleontologist Kristi Curry-Rogers told the publication Scientific American , "With nothing quite like sauropods today, our work on these creatures is akin to studying aliens."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 正如古生物学家克里斯蒂·库里-罗杰斯（Kristi Curry-Rogers）对《科学美国人》杂志所说的那样，"今天没有什么东西能像蜥脚类恐龙一样，我们对这些生物的研究类似于研究外星人。"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 上面的, 较高的, 上级的, 上院的, 穿在外面的, 北部的, 地表的, 后期的；n. 鞋帮, 上齿
vocabulary.context: It works best when paleontologists find the upper arm bone and thigh bone from the same sauropod, because those bones are the most important for an animal when it comes to movement and supporting its weight.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当古生物学家找到同一蜥脚类恐龙的上臂骨和大腿骨时，效果最好，因为这些骨头对于动物的运动和支撑体重来说是最重要的

vocabulary.definition: n. 红猫, 短尾猫
vocabulary.context: "I'm sorry, we don't do bobcat removals, ma'am.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "抱歉，我们不做山猫清除工作，女士

vocabulary.definition: adj. 滑的, 滑溜的
vocabulary.context: There are also cat-burglar bobcats, scary mountain lions, sleek deer, and slithery rattlesnakes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 还有防盗的山猫、可怕的山狮、光滑的鹿和滑溜的响尾蛇

vocabulary.definition: n. 野猪类
vocabulary.context: They look a lot like pigs but are really peccaries.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它们看起来很像猪，但实际上是西貒

vocabulary.definition: adv. 粗鲁地；无礼地；自傲地
vocabulary.context: He rises slowly, stretching insolently before stalking off in that regal cat way that makes it clear he's none too pleased his nap was interrupted.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他慢慢地站起来，傲慢地伸了个懒腰，然后以一种帝王猫的方式大步走开，这表明他对自己的午睡被打断并不太高兴

vocabulary.definition: adj. 角的, 似角一样坚硬的, 角质的, 老茧的；[医] 角性的, 角的
vocabulary.context: I collected tadpoles, frogs, horny toads, minnows, orphaned rabbits, wounded birds, and whatever else I ran across.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我收集蝌蚪、青蛙、角质蟾蜍、米诺鱼、孤兔、受伤的鸟以及我遇到的任何其他东西

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 放置架子上, 搁置；v.i. 渐渐倾斜
vocabulary.context: Let's shelve the water plan for now."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 让我们暂时搁置供水计划吧。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 栏杆, 扶手, 女儿墙, 胸墙
vocabulary.context: A tiny cat face was peering at me over the parapet surrounding the flat part of the roof.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一张小猫脸透过屋顶平坦部分周围的护墙凝视着我

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未被扰乱的, 安静的, 镇定的
vocabulary.context: Apparently, the wildlife groups considered the loss of one little dog or even two a small price to pay for leaving our wild visitors undisturbed.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 显然，野生动物组织认为失去一只甚至两只小狗只是为了让我们的野生游客不受干扰而付出的小小的代价

vocabulary.definition: n. 抢7决胜局； 线路中断器
vocabulary.context: A tiebreaker was needed.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 需要决胜局

vocabulary.definition: adj. 癌的, 似癌的, 生癌的；[医] 癌性的
vocabulary.context: Caesar had a cancerous growth on his nose and the side of his face, which he tried to hide with a scarf.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 凯撒的鼻子和脸一侧长有癌瘤，他试图用围巾隐藏起来

vocabulary.definition: adj. 桶装的,(枪,炮)有管的
vocabulary.context: Though the rain came down in torrents, the rider and his mount barreled through it.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管倾盆大雨，骑手和他的坐骑还是冲了过去

vocabulary.definition: n. 看门狗, 监察人；[化] 监控设备; 监视器
vocabulary.context: The rider feared that the sound of the horse's hoofs on the cobblestone streets would awaken someone, but loud claps of thunder covered the noise, and not even a watchdog noticed them.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 骑手担心鹅卵石街道上的马蹄声会吵醒某人，但响亮的雷声掩盖了噪音，甚至连看门狗都没有注意到

vocabulary.definition: n. 成分, 选民, 构成物；adj. 构成的, 组织的, 选举的
vocabulary.context: "As I believe the voice of my constituents and of all sensible and honest men is in favor of independence, my own judgment concurs with them.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "因为我相信我的选民和所有明智而诚实的人的声音都支持独立，所以我自己的判断也同意他们的观点

vocabulary.definition: n. 紧急, 催促；[法] 紧急, 迫切, 紧急之事
vocabulary.context: Feeling the urgency in his rider's voice, the horse stretched its legs, running harder and faster.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 马感受到骑手声音中的紧迫感，伸开双腿，跑得更猛烈、更快

vocabulary.definition: adj. 意见一致的, 无异议的
vocabulary.context: Congress agreed that the vote must be unanimous - either all for or all against independence from England.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 国会同意投票必须一致--要么全部支持，要么全部反对从英国独立

vocabulary.definition: adj. 缺席的, 不在的, 缺乏的, 漫不经心的；v.t. 使缺席
vocabulary.context: McKean knew how Caesar Rodney felt about independence, so he quickly sent a message to Rodney, the absent representative.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 麦基恩知道凯撒·罗德尼对独立的感受，所以他很快给缺席的代表罗德尼发了一条信息

vocabulary.definition: adj. 畅销的
vocabulary.context: She had not only already written a bestselling autobiography but had also earned her degree, "cum laude" (with honor), despite being both deaf and blind.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她不仅已经写了一本畅销自传，而且还获得了学位，"以优异的成绩"（以荣誉的方式），尽管她既聋又盲

vocabulary.definition: n. 聋, 不听；[医] 聋[症]
vocabulary.context: The Kellers already had brought Helen to be examined by doctors, who told them that no operation could reverse Helen's deafness and blindness.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 凯勒夫妇已经带海伦去接受医生检查，医生告诉他们，没有任何手术可以逆转海伦的耳聋和失明

vocabulary.definition: n. 畅销书
vocabulary.context: It was a bestseller that remains in print in more than 50 languages.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这是一本畅销书，至今仍以 50 多种语言印刷

vocabulary.definition: n. 发脾气, 发怒；[医] 暴怒, 发脾气
vocabulary.context: They let her run around half wild, and she threw tantrums when she did not get her way.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们让她半疯地跑来跑去，当她不按自己的意愿行事时，她就会发脾气

vocabulary.definition: n. 动物学, 动物区系；[医] 动物学
vocabulary.context: She learned arithmetic, zoology, geography, botany, and history.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她学习了算术、动物学、地理、植物学和历史

vocabulary.definition: n. 盲目, 失明, 愚昧；[医] 盲, 视觉缺失
vocabulary.context: The Kellers already had brought Helen to be examined by doctors, who told them that no operation could reverse Helen's deafness and blindness.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 凯勒夫妇已经带海伦去接受医生检查，医生告诉他们，没有任何手术可以逆转海伦的耳聋和失明

vocabulary.definition: n. 热望, 志向, 渴望；[医] 吸入; 吸[引], 吸引术
vocabulary.context: When people tried to warn Helen about her aspirations, she responded with a prediction that someday women "will all go to college, and some day they will vote.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当人们试图警告海伦她的愿望时，她做出了回应，预测有一天女性"都会上大学，有一天她们会投票

vocabulary.definition: adv. 最初, 开头
vocabulary.context: Sullivan, for her part, initially clashed with the Kellers.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 沙利文最初与凯勒夫妇发生冲突

vocabulary.definition: n. 草食动物
vocabulary.context: They reveal whether the animal that produced the poop was a herbivore (plant-eater), carnivore (meat-eater), or omnivore (eater of both plants and animals).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们揭示了产生粪便的动物是食草动物（食植物）、食肉动物（食肉动物）还是杂食动物（既吃植物又吃动物）

vocabulary.definition: n. 不偏食的人, 杂食动物
vocabulary.context: They reveal whether the animal that produced the poop was a herbivore (plant-eater), carnivore (meat-eater), or omnivore (eater of both plants and animals).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们揭示了产生粪便的动物是食草动物（食植物）、食肉动物（食肉动物）还是杂食动物（既吃植物又吃动物）

vocabulary.definition: n. 粪化石；[化] 粪化石
vocabulary.context: (Yeah, gross, but it's actually nutritious.) And on rare occasion, the animal's feces would quickly get covered by mineral-rich sediment or water and over a really long time harden into a fossil, creating what scientists call a "coprolite" and other people call a "dung stone." (Both terms are right.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: （是的，很恶心，但它实际上很有营养。）在极少数情况下，动物的粪便会很快被富含矿物质的沉积物或水覆盖，并在很长一段时间内硬化成化石，形成科学家所说的"粪化石"和其他人所说的"粪石"。 （这两个术语都是正确的

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有军刀形的, 上犬齿的
vocabulary.context: Yes, we're talking about poop - or, to be more polite, "stool," "dung," "feces," or "excrement." Call it what you will, but for many paleontologists, the scientists who study life long ago, finding poop from a dinosaur, saber-toothed cat, or even tiny sea snail is like hitting a jackpot.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 是的，我们谈论的是粪便 - 或者，更礼貌地说，"粪便"、"粪便"、"粪便"或"排泄物"。不管你怎么称呼它，但对于许多古生物学家来说，这些很久以前研究生命的科学家，从恐龙、剑齿虎、甚至小海蜗牛的粪便中发现就像中了大奖一样

vocabulary.definition: n. 排泄, 大便；[医] 粪便
vocabulary.context: Yes, we're talking about poop - or, to be more polite, "stool," "dung," "feces," or "excrement." Call it what you will, but for many paleontologists, the scientists who study life long ago, finding poop from a dinosaur, saber-toothed cat, or even tiny sea snail is like hitting a jackpot.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 是的，我们谈论的是粪便 - 或者，更礼貌地说，"粪便"、"粪便"、"粪便"或"排泄物"。不管你怎么称呼它，但对于许多古生物学家来说，这些很久以前研究生命的科学家，从恐龙、剑齿虎、甚至小海蜗牛的粪便中发现就像中了大奖一样

vocabulary.definition: n. 食人者, 吃同类的动物；adj. 食同类的, 吃人肉的
vocabulary.context: Or even a cannibal.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 甚至是食人者

vocabulary.definition: adj. 可怕的, 令人毛骨悚然的, 阴森的
vocabulary.context: News of their gruesome discovery was a big deal and published in the scientific journal Paleontology and reported by the BBC .
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们可怕的发现的消息引起了轰动，并发表在科学杂志《古生物学》上，并由英国广播公司报道

vocabulary.definition: n. 原始人, 早期艺术家；adj. 原始的, 上古的；[计] 图元; 原语; 基元
vocabulary.context: Other prehistoric sharks and primitive fish produced scroll-shaped feces that looked like a rolled sheet of paper, according to a study published in Science Reports .
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 根据发表在《科学报告》上的一项研究，其他史前鲨鱼和原始鱼类产生的粪便呈卷轴状，看起来像一张卷起来的纸

vocabulary.definition: adj. 鳄鱼的, 鳄鱼一样的；n. 鳄目动物
vocabulary.context: Besides the birds (avian dinosaurs), other survivors include some mammals, amphibians, fish, and reptiles, such as turtles, lizards, snakes, and crocodilians.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 除了鸟类（鸟类恐龙）之外，其他幸存者还包括一些哺乳动物、两栖动物、鱼类和爬行动物，例如海龟、蜥蜴、蛇和鳄鱼

vocabulary.definition: v. 集中各人智慧猛攻( brainstorm的过去式和过去分词 )
vocabulary.context: Paleontologists - the scientists who study life long ago - brainstormed lots of possible explanations.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 古生物学家--很久以前研究生命的科学家--集思广益，提出了许多可能的解释

vocabulary.definition: adj. 鸟类的；n. 鸟
vocabulary.context: The food chain collapses, taking down those non-avian dinosaurs and many other animals.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 食物链崩溃，消灭了那些非鸟类恐龙和许多其他动物

vocabulary.definition: n. 热带地区
vocabulary.context: Without the warmth of the sun, temperatures plunge for several years, causing what scientists call an "impact winter." Even in the tropics - the warmest regions of Earth - the average temperature drops from 81 degrees Fahrenheit to a chilly 41 degrees.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 没有阳光的温暖，气温会持续下降数年，导致科学家所说的"影响冬天"。即使在地球上最温暖的热带地区，平均气温也会从 81 华氏度降至寒冷的 41 度

vocabulary.definition: adj. 吓人的, 可怕的, 害怕的
vocabulary.context: The fearsome serpent-like sea reptiles (plesiosaurs, mosasaurs and ichthyosaurs) also die out, as do the pterosaurs, the mighty winged reptiles that ruled the skies during the dinosaur era.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 可怕的蛇状海洋爬行动物（蛇颈龙、沧龙和鱼龙）也灭绝了，翼龙也是如此，翼龙是恐龙时代统治天空的强大有翼爬行动物

vocabulary.definition: adj. 地理(学)的, 地区(性)的
vocabulary.context: As earthquake expert Rick Aster explained in National Geographic magazine, "[An] event of this size would be the equivalent of all the world's earthquakes for the past 160 years going off simultaneously."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 正如地震专家 Rick Aster 在《国家地理》杂志上解释的那样，"这种规模的事件相当于过去 160 年来世界上所有地震同时发生。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 意外事故, 伤亡, 受害者；[化] 事故
vocabulary.context: They are not the only casualties of the mass extinction.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们并不是大灭绝的唯一受害者

vocabulary.definition: n. 同等物, 等价物, 相等物；adj. 相等的, 相当的, 同意义的；[计] 等价的
vocabulary.context: As earthquake expert Rick Aster explained in National Geographic magazine, "[An] event of this size would be the equivalent of all the world's earthquakes for the past 160 years going off simultaneously."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 正如地震专家 Rick Aster 在《国家地理》杂志上解释的那样，"这种规模的事件相当于过去 160 年来世界上所有地震同时发生。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 诵读困难者；adj. 诵读困难的
vocabulary.context: I'm dyslexic," Tyler explained.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我有诵读困难，"泰勒解释道

vocabulary.definition: n. 诵读困难；[医] 诵读困难
vocabulary.context: "Javon, do you know what dyslexia is?" he asked, sounding annoyed.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "Javon，你知道什么是阅读障碍吗？"他问道，听起来很恼火

vocabulary.definition: n. 计划人, 发起人, 规划人, 放映机, 映画器, 幻灯机, 幻灯, 探照灯, 发射器；[计] 投影仪
vocabulary.context: T was writing on the projector, same as always.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: T 一如既往地在投影仪上书写

vocabulary.definition: n. 自拍照
vocabulary.context: Sometimes his friends couldn't figure out how to hold their phones to fit him and everyone else in a selfie.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 有时，他的朋友们不知道如何握住手机以适合他和其他人的自拍

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 插嘴, 突然插入
vocabulary.context: "Because he's short," Tyler interjected.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "因为他很矮，"泰勒插话道

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 涂鸦, 混时间；n. 傻子
vocabulary.context: Kids were taking notes or doodling, same as always.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 孩子们一如既往地做笔记或涂鸦

vocabulary.definition: adv. 枯燥无味地, 完全地, 直截了当地, 毅然地, 断然地
vocabulary.context: "But it's not fair." Javon said flatly.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "但这不公平。"贾文淡淡的说道

vocabulary.definition: n. 爆发, 突发, 破裂, 暴乱
vocabulary.context: T looked surprised at Javon's outburst.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: T 对 Javon 的爆发感到惊讶

vocabulary.definition: n. 鱼类学者, 鱼类研究者
vocabulary.context: Adjany Costa is an ichthyologist [ik-thee-ol-uh-jist].
vocabulary.contextTranslation: Adjany Costa 是一位鱼类学家 [ik-thee-ol-uh-jist]

vocabulary.definition: adj. 凝胶状的；[医] 胶状的
vocabulary.context: Or the blobfish, rightfully named the world's ugliest fish, with its gelatinous face and drooping nose.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 还有水滴鱼，它有着凝胶状的脸和下垂的鼻子，被正确地称为世界上最丑的鱼

vocabulary.definition: n. 鱼类学；[医] 鱼类学
vocabulary.context: Thus, despite her love of nature, ichthyology did not even cross her mind.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因此，尽管她热爱自然，但她根本没有想过鱼类学

vocabulary.definition: adj. 使人畏惧的
vocabulary.context: It was awe-inspiring for me," Rosenbaum said in an interview with Yale's Graduate School of Arts and Sciences.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这对我来说是令人敬畏的，"罗森鲍姆在接受耶鲁大学艺术与科学研究生院采访时说道

vocabulary.definition: adj. 牵强的, 未必会的; 不着边际
vocabulary.context: In her home country of Angola, oil and gas companies dominate the economy, so studying biology felt like a far-fetched dream.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在她的祖国安哥拉，石油和天然气公司主导着经济，因此学习生物学感觉像是一个遥不可及的梦想

vocabulary.definition: adj. 丑陋的, 邪恶的, 险恶的, 不祥的；n. 丑陋的人(或物)
vocabulary.context: Or the blobfish, rightfully named the world's ugliest fish, with its gelatinous face and drooping nose.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 还有水滴鱼，它有着凝胶状的脸和下垂的鼻子，被正确地称为世界上最丑的鱼

vocabulary.definition: adj. 能适应的, 适合的, 可修改的
vocabulary.context: Diatoms are very adaptable.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 硅藻的适应性很强

vocabulary.definition: n. 制造, 生产, 产物；[医] 产生, 生成
vocabulary.context: Meeda analyzes how diatoms' ability to respond rapidly to their environment could be used in health care, food production, or conservation.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: Meeda 分析了硅藻对其环境快速反应的能力如何用于医疗保健、食品生产或保护

vocabulary.definition: n. （发射宇宙飞船等的）倒数读秒, 倒计时( countdown的复数形式 ); 大事临近的时期
vocabulary.context: ...reserve the launch date and collaborates with engineers and scientists to make sure take-offs go smoothly. Baez also manages launch countdowns. 5-4-3-2-1-blast-off!
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...保留发射日期并与工程师和科学家合作，确保起飞顺利进行。贝兹还管理发射倒计时。 5-4-3-2-1-起飞！

vocabulary.definition: n. 发射
vocabulary.context: ...and collaborates with engineers and scientists to make sure take-offs go smoothly. Baez also manages launch countdowns. 5-4-3-2-1-blast-off!
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...并与工程师和科学家合作，确保起飞顺利进行。贝兹还管理发射倒计时。 5-4-3-2-1-起飞！

vocabulary.definition: n. 油田
vocabulary.context: This scene played out in the 1998 space movie Armageddon , where NASA works with oil-field drillers to blow up an asteroid before it strikes Earth.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这一场景在 1998 年的太空电影《世界末日》中上演，美国宇航局与油田钻探人员合作，在一颗小行星撞击地球之前将其炸毁

vocabulary.definition: adj. 假设的, 有待证实的, 假定的, 有前提的；[计] 理想的
vocabulary.context: "The results from the DART mission are going to inform our hypothetical work on planetary defense going forward," said aerospace engineer Barbee.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "DART 任务的结果将为我们未来的行星防御假设工作提供信息，"航空航天工程师巴比说

vocabulary.definition: v. 不完全地陈述, 保守地说, 有意轻描淡写
vocabulary.context: "It's going to be affecting the way we think about planetary defense mission design for many years to come, so the importance of the mission in that regard really can't be understated."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "这将影响我们在未来许多年对行星防御任务设计的思考方式，因此该任务在这方面的重要性确实不能被低估。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 同等, 协调；[计] 协调; 进程协调
vocabulary.context: So, in 2016, NASA established the Planetary Defense Coordination Office to hunt asteroids that could threaten Earth before they become a problem.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因此，2016年，美国宇航局成立了行星防御协调办公室，以在可能威胁地球的小行星成为问题之前搜寻它们

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 合作, 勾结；[法] 合作, 合著, 通敌
vocabulary.context: He works with governments and companies to reserve the launch date and collaborates with engineers and scientists to make sure take-offs go smoothly.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他与政府和公司合作预定发射日期，并与工程师和科学家合作确保起飞顺利进行

vocabulary.definition: adj. 技术的；[经] 工艺的, 技术的
vocabulary.context: That's why NASA's Planetary Defense Coordination Office is developing technological weapons to defend against NEOs that could threaten Earth in the future.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这就是为什么美国宇航局行星防御协调办公室正在开发技术武器来防御未来可能威胁地球的近地天体

vocabulary.definition: n. 浴缸

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我小心翼翼地埋葬了它

vocabulary.definition: n. 小猫, 淘气姑娘；v.t. 产仔, 卖弄风情
vocabulary.context: When the kitten ripped the curtain,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当小猫撕开窗帘时，

vocabulary.context: my pen leaked on my shirt,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我的笔漏到了衬衫上，

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我坐在我的甜点上

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 很快，比火箭快得多，

vocabulary.definition: n. 裂痕, 破绽, 拉裂, 浪子, 巨浪；v.i. 被拉开, 裂开, 猛冲；v.t. 撕, 扯, 劈
vocabulary.context: When the kitten ripped the curtain,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当小猫撕开窗帘时，

vocabulary.definition: n. 创伤, 伤口, 伤疤, 伤害, 痛苦；v.t. 伤害, 损害, 使受伤；v.i. 打伤, 伤害；wind的过去式和过去分词
vocabulary.context: and my wounds have almost healed.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我的伤口几乎痊愈了

vocabulary.definition: adj. 幕后的
vocabulary.context: A lot of the work that usually goes on behind-the-scenes is open at the Wyoming Dinosaur Center.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 许多通常在幕后进行的工作都是在怀俄明州恐龙中心开放的

vocabulary.definition: adj. 红褐色的；n. 红褐色
vocabulary.context: These days it's a hilly area covered in reddish-brown dirt and rock.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如今，这里是一片丘陵地区，覆盖着红棕色的泥土和岩石

vocabulary.definition: n. 鹤嘴锄, 镐；v.t. vi. 用鹤嘴锄掘
vocabulary.context: Big tools like shovels and pickaxes are used to remove top layers of dirt and rock.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 铲子和镐等大型工具用于清除表层泥土和岩石

vocabulary.definition: n. 牙科医生；[医] 牙医师
vocabulary.context: Did you know that tools exactly like the ones your dentist works with are used on fossils?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 您是否知道在化石上使用的工具与您的牙医使用的工具完全相同？

vocabulary.definition: v. 挖空, 挖出, 凿通
vocabulary.context: Children and adults can both help excavate, or uncover, fossils.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 儿童和成人都可以帮助挖掘或发现化石

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 发掘, 掘出, 从洞中赶出, 揭露, 发现
vocabulary.context: Shouts of joy and surprise burst from around the site as volunteers exclaim as they unearth a fossil.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当志愿者挖掘化石时，现场周围爆发出喜悦和惊讶的叫喊声

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 把...存档；n. 档案馆, 档案文件；[计] 挡案库, 存档
vocabulary.context: It will be recorded in the Wyoming Dinosaur Center archive.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它将被记录在怀俄明州恐龙中心档案中

vocabulary.definition: n. 志愿者；adj. 志愿的；v. 自愿
vocabulary.context: Shouts of joy and surprise burst from around the site as volunteers exclaim as they unearth a fossil.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当志愿者挖掘化石时，现场周围爆发出喜悦和惊讶的叫喊声

vocabulary.definition: n. 数百万
vocabulary.context: It's exciting to discover something that has been buried for many millions of years.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 发现一些被埋藏了数百万年的东西是令人兴奋的

vocabulary.definition: n. 泄殖腔, 阴沟；[医] 泄殖腔, 一穴肛; 骨瘘
vocabulary.context: OK, if you want to get all scientific about it, the proper term is "cloacal vent." The vent is the opening under the animal's tail, and the cloaca (kloh-EY-kuh) is the muscular chamber, or space, behind it.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 好吧，如果你想获得有关它的所有科学信息，正确的术语是"泄殖腔出口"。排气口是动物尾巴下方的开口，泄殖腔 (kloh-EY-kuh) 是其后面的肌肉室或空间

vocabulary.definition: adj. 阴沟, 厕所, 下水道, 泄殖腔；[医] 泄殖腔的
vocabulary.context: OK, if you want to get all scientific about it, the proper term is "cloacal vent." The vent is the opening under the animal's tail, and the cloaca (kloh-EY-kuh) is the muscular chamber, or space, behind it.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 好吧，如果你想获得有关它的所有科学信息，正确的术语是"泄殖腔出口"。排气口是动物尾巴下方的开口，泄殖腔 (kloh-EY-kuh) 是其后面的肌肉室或空间

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有刚毛的, 如刚毛的, 易怒的
vocabulary.context: It was about the size of a golden retriever, had horns on the sides of its head, and sported a bristly tail.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它的大小与金毛猎犬差不多，头部两侧长有角，尾巴上有刚毛

vocabulary.definition: n. (英)便士, 小便；v.i. 小便
vocabulary.context: Like other reptiles, Psittacosaurus's cloaca did everything the dino needed to do: peeing, pooping, laying eggs, you name it.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 和其他爬行动物一样，鹦鹉嘴龙的泄殖腔可以完成恐龙需要做的一切：撒尿、拉便、产卵，凡是你能想到的

vocabulary.definition: n. 背部, 背后, 后方
vocabulary.context: The little dino's backside was not shaped like any other animal's - and the researchers checked.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 研究人员对此进行了检查，发现这只小恐龙的背部形状与其他动物不同

vocabulary.definition: n. 排气口, 出口, 通风孔, 发泄；v.t. 发泄, 排出, 给...开孔
vocabulary.context: Kelly, a member of the research team and an expert on animals' rear ends, told the New York Times that most cloacal vents just look like slits or rounded holes, sometimes with wrinkly skin or scales around them.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 研究小组成员、动物臀部专家凯利告诉《纽约时报》，大多数泄殖腔泄殖腔看起来就像狭缝或圆孔，有时周围有皱纹的皮肤或鳞片

vocabulary.definition: n. 相似, 类似；[计] 模拟
vocabulary.context: Paleontologist Jakob Vinther, who studied the rear, used an analogy to explain its multipurpose wonders to Popular Science magazine: "It's the Swiss army knife of back ends."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 研究后部的古生物学家雅各布·温瑟 (Jakob Vinther) 用一个类比向《大众科学》杂志解释了其多功能奇迹："它是后端的瑞士军刀。"

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 重建, 改造；[化] 按原样修复
vocabulary.context: It was fossilized and as hard as a rock.) They asked artist Robert Nicholls, who specializes in dinosaurs, to draw Psittacosaurus and reconstruct a 3D model of the dino.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它已经变成化石，坚硬如岩石。）他们请专门研究恐龙的艺术家罗伯特·尼科尔斯绘制鹦鹉嘴龙并重建恐龙的 3D 模型

vocabulary.definition: n. 释放, 发泄, 豁免, 发行, 释放证书；v.t. 释放, 解除, 放松, 豁免, 免除, 发布, 放弃, 让与；n. 发布；[计] 版本, 发布
vocabulary.context: The researchers think Psittacosaurus may have been able to release its own dino-pleasing perfume, too.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 研究人员认为鹦鹉嘴龙也可能能够释放自己的令恐龙愉悦的香水

vocabulary.definition: adj. 弯弯曲曲的
vocabulary.context: It is a 25,000-mile squiggly upside down U curving around the top of the Pacific Ocean from New Zealand to Japan, around the coast of western North America, down to the tip of South America.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它是一条全长 25,000 英里的蜿蜒倒 U 字形曲线，从新西兰到日本，环绕太平洋顶部，绕过北美西部海岸，一直延伸到南美洲尖端

vocabulary.definition: n. 重叠, 重复, 部分的同时发生；v.t. 重叠, 重复, 与...同时发生；v.i. 迭盖, 部分的同时发生；[计] 重叠
vocabulary.context: The area of the Ring of Fire has an almost unbroken series of subduction zones because so many tectonic plates overlap there, which is why 76% of the earth's seismic energy is released there.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 火环地区有一系列几乎不间断的俯冲带，因为有如此多的构造板块重叠在那里，这就是为什么地球76%的地震能量都在那里释放的原因

vocabulary.definition: adj. 地质的, 地质学的
vocabulary.context: Together, these geologic features are what make this area so unstable.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些地质特征共同导致该地区如此不稳定

vocabulary.definition: adj. 地震的
vocabulary.context: It is the seismic (vibration) waves caused by this energy that create an earthquake.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 正是这种能量引起的地震（振动）波引发了地震

vocabulary.definition: n. 叶, 植物体；[医] 叶(指蕨类、棕榈类、苏铁类)
vocabulary.context: Palm trees bending in the breeze as the warm sun shines through the green fronds.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当温暖的阳光透过绿色的叶子照射时，棕榈树在微风中弯曲

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未破损的, 未被征服的, 未打垮的, 不屈服的, 不涣散的, 整齐的, 未中断的；[法] 未被阻断的, 未破损的, 完好的
vocabulary.context: The area of the Ring of Fire has an almost unbroken series of subduction zones because so many tectonic plates overlap there, which is why 76% of the earth's seismic energy is released there.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 火环地区有一系列几乎不间断的俯冲带，因为有如此多的构造板块重叠在那里，这就是为什么地球76%的地震能量都在那里释放的原因

vocabulary.definition: n. 上侧, 上段, 上部；[机] 上侧, 上部
vocabulary.context: It is a 25,000-mile squiggly upside down U curving around the top of the Pacific Ocean from New Zealand to Japan, around the coast of western North America, down to the tip of South America.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它是一条全长 25,000 英里的蜿蜒倒 U 字形曲线，从新西兰到日本，环绕太平洋顶部，绕过北美西部海岸，一直延伸到南美洲尖端

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 产生, 发生, 导致；[电] 产生
vocabulary.context: They monitor earthquake activity and any tsunami waves that might be generated and send out warnings.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们监测地震活动和可能产生的任何海啸并发出警告

vocabulary.definition: adj. 令人讨厌的, 难以下咽的
vocabulary.context: Cultures where insects are thought of as yucky will also need to change their minds - maybe by starting with cricket powder mixed into food instead of eating the bugs themselves.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 那些认为昆虫令人厌恶的文化也需要改变他们的想法--也许可以从将蟋蟀粉混合到食物中开始，而不是吃虫子本身

vocabulary.definition: n. 饱嗝儿, 打嗝；v. 打饱嗝
vocabulary.context: In a cow, the methane goes out when it burps.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 牛打嗝时，甲烷就会消失

vocabulary.definition: n. 蝗虫, 蚱蜢, 蝉
vocabulary.context: Insects such as mealworms, crickets, and locusts release 80 to 100 times less methane than cattle.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 粉虫、蟋蟀和蝗虫等昆虫释放的甲烷比牛少 80 至 100 倍

vocabulary.definition: n. 甲烷, 沼气；[化] 甲烷; 沼气
vocabulary.context: As the body breaks down food, gases such as methane and carbon dioxide are released.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当身体分解食物时，会释放出甲烷和二氧化碳等气体

vocabulary.definition: n. 消化力, 领悟；[化] 消化; 消化作用; 蒸煮; 蒸煮作用; 煮解; 浸提; 陈化
vocabulary.context: Passing gas is a natural result of digestion.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 排气是消化的自然结果

vocabulary.definition: n. 汉堡包（等于hamburger）
vocabulary.context: Picture your family's last trip to a burger restaurant.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 想象一下你的家人最后一次去汉堡店的情景

vocabulary.definition: n. 仓库, 货栈, 大商店；v.t. 储入仓库
vocabulary.context: Most insects can be raised anywhere because they don't need wide, open spaces to roam - a cricket "farm" is really a warehouse filled with thousands of bins where the crickets live.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 大多数昆虫可以在任何地方饲养，因为它们不需要宽阔、开放的空间来漫游--蟋蟀"农场"实际上是一个装满了数千个箱子的仓库，蟋蟀生活在那里

vocabulary.definition: n. 餐馆, 饭店
vocabulary.context: Picture your family's last trip to a burger restaurant.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 想象一下你的家人最后一次去汉堡店的情景

vocabulary.definition: adv. 确切地, 精确地, 恰好, 完全地, 确实, 恰恰正是, 确实如此
vocabulary.context: It's possible the future may bring exactly that.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 未来可能正是如此

vocabulary.definition: n. [动]反荫蔽(动物保护色的一种类型, 这类动物背部颜色深于腹部,上面投下的光线使它全身颜色均匀而不醒目)
vocabulary.context: Paleontologists recently found rare dino fossils with well-preserved skin - one of a horse-sized ankylosaur called Borealopelta and another of a pony-sized ceratopsian called Psittacosaurus - which showed they were light-colored on the bottom and darker on the top, a camouflage pattern known as countershading.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 古生物学家最近发现了皮肤保存完好的稀有恐龙化石 - 一个是马大小的甲龙，称为 Borealopelta，另一个是小马大小的角龙，称为 Psittacosaurus - 这表明它们的底部颜色较浅，顶部颜色较深，这是一种称为反阴影的伪装图案

vocabulary.definition: n. 羚羊的一种
vocabulary.context: Many plant-eating dinosaurs - including Stegosaurus , horned-and-frilled ceratopsians and others - found safety in numbers, eating and traveling in herds, like modern-day wildebeest or springbok antelope.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 许多食草恐龙--包括剑龙、有角和褶边的角龙等--在数量众多、成群进食和旅行时找到了安全感，就像现代的角马或跳羚一样

vocabulary.definition: n. 跳羚
vocabulary.context: Many plant-eating dinosaurs - including Stegosaurus , horned-and-frilled ceratopsians and others - found safety in numbers, eating and traveling in herds, like modern-day wildebeest or springbok antelope.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 许多食草恐龙--包括剑龙、有角和褶边的角龙等--在数量众多、成群进食和旅行时找到了安全感，就像现代的角马或跳羚一样

vocabulary.definition: n. (白垩纪的古生物)甲龙(因披甲而有"爬行坦克" 之称)
vocabulary.context: Paleontologists recently found rare dino fossils with well-preserved skin - one of a horse-sized ankylosaur called Borealopelta and another of a pony-sized ceratopsian called Psittacosaurus - which showed they were light-colored on the bottom and darker on the top, a camouflage pattern known as countershading.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 古生物学家最近发现了皮肤保存完好的稀有恐龙化石 - 一个是马大小的甲龙，称为 Borealopelta，另一个是小马大小的角龙，称为 Psittacosaurus - 这表明它们的底部颜色较浅，顶部颜色较深，这是一种称为反阴影的伪装图案

vocabulary.definition: adj. & adv. 头先向前的（地）
vocabulary.context: It was easy to picture Pachycephalosaurs charging head-first into predators or rivals - much like bighorn rams butting heads.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 很容易想象厚头龙头朝下冲向掠食者或竞争对手--就像大角公羊撞头一样

vocabulary.definition: n. 公羊, 撞锤；v.i. 猛击, 撞；v.t. 猛击, 填塞, 反复灌输；n. 只读内存, 随机存取内存, 随机存取存储器；[计] 随机存取存储器
vocabulary.context: Scientists haven't found scars suggesting the dinos rammed into things.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 科学家们还没有发现恐龙撞击物体的伤痕

vocabulary.definition: n. 使者, 传令官, 先驱；v.t. 预报, 宣布, 传达, 欢呼
vocabulary.context: "You have these iconic images of Triceratops doing battle with Tyrannosaurus rex ," paleontologist Caleb Brown told the Canadian newspaper Calgary Herald .
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "你可以看到这些三角龙与霸王龙战斗的标志性图像，"古生物学家迦勒·布朗告诉加拿大报纸《卡尔加里先驱报》

vocabulary.definition: n. 对手, 竞争者；adj. 竞争的
vocabulary.context: The ceratopsians' horns may have added to their dashing looks in the eyes of potential mates, but they also probably helped the dinosaurs fight off rivals - much like two male antelope will lock horns and push each other back and forth until one gives up and leaves.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 角龙的角可能让它们在潜在配偶眼中显得英俊潇洒，但它们也可能帮助恐龙击退竞争对手--就像两只雄性羚羊会锁住角并互相推挤，直到其中一只放弃并离开

vocabulary.definition: n. 雷龙(古生物)
vocabulary.context: Between the two of them, they discovered Triceratops , Stegosaurus , Brontosaurus , Coelophysis and many other extinct animals.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们两人之间，发现了三角龙、剑龙、雷龙、腔骨龙等多种灭绝动物

vocabulary.definition: n. 饮者, 酒徒
vocabulary.context: Edward Drinker Cope and Othniel Charles Marsh were brilliant paleontologists of the late 1800s.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 爱德华·德林克·科普 (Edward Drinker Cope) 和奥塞尼尔·查尔斯·马什 (Othniel Charles Marsh) 是 1800 年代末杰出的古生物学家

vocabulary.definition: n. 自然主义者
vocabulary.context: Then, in 1877, he took the bold step of buying a scientific journal, The American Naturalist , so he could publish his discoveries in it.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然后，在 1877 年，他采取了大胆的步骤，购买了一份科学期刊《美国博物学家》，这样他就可以在上面发表他的发现

vocabulary.definition: adj. 地质学的, 地质的
vocabulary.context: Geological Survey, which gave him the power to make sure Cope's work didn't receive any government funding - a source of support that both scientists had relied on.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 地质调查局，这使他有权确保科普的工作不会得到任何政府资助--这是两位科学家都依赖的支持来源

vocabulary.definition: n. 竞争, 对抗；[医] 拮抗
vocabulary.context: With that newspaper article, one of the most bitter rivalries in the history of paleontology - and maybe all of science - burst out into the open.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 随着那篇报纸文章的出现，古生物学史上--也许是整个科学史上--最激烈的竞争之一爆发了

vocabulary.definition: n. 贿赂；v.t. 贿赂, 收买；v.i. 行贿
vocabulary.context: Behind Cope's back, Marsh bribed the miners to send the fossils directly to him at Yale University instead of to Cope.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 马什背着科普贿赂矿工，让他们将化石直接寄给他在耶鲁大学，而不是科普

vocabulary.definition: n. 虚荣心, 虚无, 自负, 空虚, 时髦的小摆饰
vocabulary.context: "When I informed Professor Cope of it, his wounded vanity received a shock from which it has never recovered, and he has since been my bitter enemy," Marsh wrote in an 1890 letter to the New York Herald newspaper.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "当我把这件事告诉科普教授时，他受伤的虚荣心受到了打击，再也没有恢复过来，从此他就成了我的死敌，"马什在 1890 年写给《纽约先驱报》的信中写道

vocabulary.definition: n. 电报, 一封电报；[计] 电报
vocabulary.context: Early on, he announced his findings via telegram - the only way to instantly communicate information back then.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 早些时候，他通过电报宣布了他的发现--这是当时即时传达信息的唯一方式
